synthesis.module_inventory_summary: cl_revenue_ops is a ~58k-LOC single-process Python CLN plugin: a 10,378-line entrypoint (cl-revenue-ops.py: 119 options, 36+ operator RPCs, 4 notification subscriptions, 8 daemon loops, ThreadSafeRpcProxy, bootstrap/shutdown) plus 46 modules. Major clusters: (1) foundations — utils (msat parsing + directional rounding: ceil for fees/costs/revenue, floor for balances, toward-zero for deltas), config.py (~150-field Config + validation tables + immutable ConfigSnapshot + DB-persisted overrides with 3-layer precedence), database.py (7,962 LOC; ~35 tables, WAL, per-thread connections, additive versionless migrations, BEGIN IMMEDIATE budget-reservation ledger, msat/sat dual-column convention), data_service.py (tiered TTL RPC cache: forever/300s/30s/never); (2) governed economics layer (~2.4k LOC across econ_types/reason_codes/econ_snapshot/cycle_context/econ_intents/econ_arbiter/governor_facade/econ_ledger/econ_reconcile/econ_ev/econ_cycle/econ_shadow + 7 frozen JSON schemas + a 40-scenario conformance corpus) — explicitly built as the language-neutral port boundary: checked integer money math, canonical JSON idempotency keys, deterministic arbitration, append-only ledger, fail-closed governor / fail-open shadow asymmetry; (3) fee control stack (fee_controller.py 8.6k LOC: discounted Gaussian Thompson sampling with Bayesian quadratic regression + Cholesky draws, PI inventory controller, Vegas mempool-spike floor, neighbor-gossip market intelligence, chain-cost/rebalance floors, congestion episodes, zero-flow ratchet, v2_state_json per-channel persistence, admission_policy htlcmax valve pinned by golden fixtures); (4) rebalance stack (~8.5k LOC across 15 rebalance_* files: pure pair planner, askrene v3 router with throwaway exclude layers, native sendpay/waitsendpay executor with payment_pending safety semantics, engine with sats-EV gate, futility/cooldowns, unified budget reservations, defibrillator diagnostic, segment observations); (5) capital allocation (capacity_planner 4.2k, capex_budget, capital_efficiency, boltz_manager 2.7k subprocess wrapper + in-entrypoint auto-cycle, lnplus_swaps 2.1k HTTP automation with irreversible-obligation state machine); (6) analytics/policy (profitability_analyzer, flow_analysis, classification, demand_flow, policy_manager, protection_service, risk_profiles, growth_budget). A 3,091-test suite plus golden fixtures (htlcmax, close_protection) and expected-projections encode the behavioral contracts; error strings, RPC response dict shapes, and datastore telemetry payloads are all substring/shape-matched public contracts.
dependency_order: config (Config struct, validation tables, ConfigSnapshot, LiquidityBuckets/ChainCostDefaults)
database connection layer (WAL, PRAGMAs, BEGIN IMMEDIATE, thread model)
schema-init-and-migrations (additive idempotent, in-place adoption of production DB)
rpc-timeout layer (cln-rpc wrapper replacing ThreadSafeRpcProxy; error taxonomy RPCTimeoutError/RPCOverloadedError)
option-registration-and-config (119 options, dynamic setconfig, revenue-config override precedence)
econ_arbiter (J3 ladder batch arbitration + live registry)
econ_cycle + econ_shadow (deterministic shadow cycle, fail-open hub)
market intelligence (neighbor gossip) + floors and safety rails + drain bias helpers
fee execution (set_channel_fee) + governor authorization for fees
RebalanceEngineV2 (sats-EV gate, futility/cooldowns, reservations, reconcile sweep)
capital_efficiency + capex_budget (unified capex engine, boltz reservation lifecycle)
plugin-bootstrap-and-init + shutdown (startup order, forward hydration, SIGTERM/cancellation)

proposed_phases.phase: Phase 1 — Skeleton, foundations, and read-only observer plugin
proposed_phases.goal: A loadable cln-plugin binary that registers all 119 revenue-ops-* options (string-tolerant parsing, identical names so operator config files load unchanged), adopts an existing production revenue_ops.db in place (connection layer, PRAGMAs, additive migrations, validation helpers), wires the timeout-wrapped cln-rpc layer + DataService TTL cache, ingests live forward/connect/disconnect/channel_state_changed notifications with INSERT OR IGNORE dedup + startup hydration (_compute_forward_hydration_start semantics), and serves the read-only RPC surface: revenue-status, revenue-health (heartbeat registry), revenue-history, revenue-report, revenue-dashboard, revenue-list-ignored/banned, revenue-config get. Includes utils rounding helpers and the Config/ConfigSnapshot system with DB-override precedence.
components: database connection layer + migrations + validation helpers
proposed_phases.exit_criteria: Plugin loads on CLN v24.11.1+ against a copy of the production DB; hydration + live forward ingestion race safely (dedup verified); read-only RPC responses are field-for-field identical to Python output on the same DB snapshot (diff harness); Python migration then Rust open (and vice versa) both succeed — schema round-trip proven; clean shutdown under 10s with a wedged RPC simulated.

proposed_phases.phase: Phase 2 — Governed economic core with byte-parity conformance
proposed_phases.goal: Port the layer explicitly designed for this port: econ_types checked integer math, reason_codes, canonical JSON (byte-identical to Python json.dumps(sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=False)), cycle_context.derive_seed, intent envelopes + idempotency keys, J3-ladder batch arbiter + live ActiveIntentRegistry (both conflict semantics), fail-closed GovernorFacade with authority ladder, append-only econ_ledger.db + replay, econ_reconcile, deterministic shadow cycle, and econ_shadow fail-open hub. Expose revenue-econ-snapshot/-cycle/-reconcile RPCs.
proposed_phases.exit_criteria: The 40-scenario tests/conformance corpus passes byte-for-byte via tools/conformance/validate_fixtures.py against Rust output; idempotency keys, intent_ids, and arbitration wire output identical to Python for identical inputs regardless of input order; ledger replay of a copied production econ_ledger.db reconstructs identical LedgerState; governor decision order (paused -> authority -> stale -> conflict -> budget) pinned by tests including fail-closed-on-panic.

proposed_phases.phase: Phase 3 — Flow analysis, profitability, policy, and the spend-budget rail
proposed_phases.goal: Make the plugin analytically complete and money-safe: flow classification (source/sink/balanced + Kalman state), profitability analyzer with msat-native P&L and the frozen [revenue, profitability-summary] datastore contract, policy_manager + protection_service, the unified BEGIN IMMEDIATE budget-reservation/spend-ledger core with terminal-state resurrection guard and stale-reservation sweeps, plus the flow-analysis, startup-snapshot, and financial-snapshot scheduler loops and the spend-ledger RPC surface (revenue-spend-*, revenue-total-cost-budget, revenue-analyze, revenue-profitability, revenue-policy).
proposed_phases.exit_criteria: reserve/spend/release lifecycle passes concurrency tests (no double-spend across restart; committed-total formula exact incl. P4-017 no-time-filter reservations); profitability-summary datastore payload validates against REVENUE_PROFITABILITY_SUMMARY_CONTRACT.md and matches Python on a DB snapshot; classification parity on fixture snapshots (one classification authority per cycle); rounding regression suite (ceil/floor/toward-zero) green.

proposed_phases.phase: Phase 4 — Fee control stack, dry-run first
proposed_phases.goal: Port the DTS+PID fee controller with all safety rails in the frozen ADR-001 order (rails -> rate-limit -> deadband -> cooldown): GaussianThompsonState with legacy v2_state_json round-trip, PIDState, VegasReflexState, neighbor-gossip market intelligence, chain-cost/rebalance/class-aware floors, congestion episodes, zero-flow ratchet, drain bias, admission_policy htlcmax valve, set_channel_fee execution with clamp contracts, failed-forward WIRE_FEE_INSUFFICIENT nudges, governed SET_FEE authorization, and the fee-adjustment loop + revenue-fee-debug/-set-fee/-fee-cycle/-profile-preview RPCs. Ship behind dry_run until parity is proven.
proposed_phases.exit_criteria: htlcmax golden fixtures byte-identical; production v2_state_json blobs (incl. legacy thompson_aimd_v1, 5-/6-tuple observations) round-trip losslessly; dry-run decision-parity harness over recorded cycles shows identical bounded/blended/damped/gossip-gated targets given identical sampled values (RNG injected); all 15 fee behavioral contracts from the lens map covered by ported tests; live cutover on the fee loop only, watched against revenue_validation.yaml rollback thresholds.

proposed_phases.phase: Phase 5 — Rebalance stack: askrene pricing, native execution, defibrillator
proposed_phases.goal: Port the full circular-rebalance pipeline: pure RebalancePlanner (first: golden parity), RebalanceRouterV3 with askrene getroutes (45s timeout), throwaway exclude-layer lifecycle and orphan sweep, NativeRouteExecutor with strict route validation and payment_pending safety semantics, RebalanceEngineV2 (sats-EV hold-margin gate, futility/per-kind cooldowns, probability-adjusted ceilings, unified-budget reservations passing the FULL budget, pending-settlement reconcile via listsendpays, concurrent execution with counted inflight-dest map), EVRebalancer facade with capital controls and inbound-fee estimation, defibrillator diagnostic, segment observations + datastore export, rebalance-check loop, and revenue-rebalance*/-rebalance-debug/-clear-reservations RPCs.
components: rebalance-check loop + budget provider closures (trait objects)
proposed_phases.exit_criteria: Planner golden-file parity (incl. round-half-even and stable sort ties); all error-string contracts ('local_budget_block', 'engine_busy', 'route_pricing_failed:', 'payment_pending_timeout:', etc.) substring-exact; pending-payment restart test: reservation held, reconcile sweep settles from listsendpays, no double-spend and no invoice deletion while pending; budget blocks recorded as 'skipped' without futility strikes; live on regtest/small budget, then production with daily budget clamp.

proposed_phases.phase: Phase 6 — Capital allocation (planner/capex/Boltz/LN+), full RPC surface, and cutover
proposed_phases.goal: Port the remaining money-committing subsystems behind their fail-closed defaults: CapexBudgetEngine (msat-internal, db_degraded fail-closed), CapacityPlanner (zombie/defibrillator flow, dead-capital stage machine, EV-gated opens/closes, recycle nominations, portfolio governor), BoltzCliManager (tokio::process + sudo argv, error-substring capability cache, journal, layered budget gates) + BoltzAutoCycle, LN+ swap automation (irreversible-obligation state machine, one-strike breaker, restart-safe), growth_budget, and the complete remaining RPC surface (planner, boltz, lnplus, expansion treasury). Regenerate config templates; run the Rust plugin in shadow/dry-run on the production node per refactor.md, then cut over.
proposed_phases.exit_criteria: close_protection golden fixtures pass; planner and boltz cycles produce identical dry-run action plans to Python on shared DB snapshots; LN+ state machine survives kill -9 mid-obligation in test; full 36-RPC response-shape diff vs Python is empty; revenue_validation.yaml harness green for the rollback window (no >3 restarts/24h, no 25% revenue drop, rebalance success >=50%) running Rust live; Python plugin retired.

risk_register: Float math parity: Python round() is banker's rounding (econ_ev, Micro clamps pin it), int() truncates toward zero, and the DTS stack does hand-rolled 3x3 Sarrus determinant/inverse/Cholesky with specific non-PD degradation paths — Rust must reimplement these numerically identically, not via nalgebra defaults; random.gauss/random.random streams cannot be reproduced, so parity tests must inject sampled values and compare the deterministic pipeline around them.

Threading-model translation: the fee controller holds one lock across DB I/O for a whole cycle (I-14) and the forward_event hook mutates Thompson state under the same lock — the tokio port must preserve those atomicity windows (single-owner actor per subsystem) or introduce torn-state bugs; rebalance execution relies on abandoned-but-running workers after a 120s as_completed ceiling whose reserve+pay critical section must NEVER be cancelled mid-payment.

SQLite schema compatibility: the port must adopt existing production DBs in place — versionless additive migrations (schema_version is write-only by design), COALESCE(msat, sat*1000) dual-column reads with budget enforcement reading cost_sats only, BEGIN IMMEDIATE as the sole cross-writer TOCTOU defense, per-chunk aggregate+delete atomicity in cleanup, and rusqlite's transaction defaults differing from Python sqlite3 autocommit (isolation_level=None) — every write path's transaction boundary needs an explicit audit.

askrene/sendpay integration: 45s getroutes timeout vs the 15s global ceiling, throwaway exclude-layer lifecycle with unique names + orphan sweep + 'Unknown layer' cache teardown, msat fields arriving as int or 'Nmsat' strings, waitsendpay code 200 = pending, and the payment_pending contract (never delete invoice/payment, hold reservation, reconcile later) — mistakes here lose real sats or double-pay; CLN v24.11.1 floor must be enforced at init.

Contract-string and response-shape drift: dozens of error strings ('local_budget_block...', 'governor_block: {code}', 'chanIds are not supported for cln', 'not a public runtime control', 'RPC timeout for method: {m}') are substring-matched by other modules, tests, and cl-hive tooling; 36 RPC handlers must accept named AND positional params with Python-default parity and emit verbatim dict shapes — needs an automated Python-vs-Rust response diff harness, not manual review.

Canonical-JSON byte parity: idempotency keys and the conformance corpus hash json.dumps(sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=False) — serde_json needs BTreeMap ordering plus care that no floats enter canonicalized payloads (integer-only by design; verify) or Python/Rust float formatting diverges silently.

v2_state_json legacy round-trip: ~50KB/channel opaque blobs with 5-/6-tuple observations, legacy thompson_aimd_v1 layouts, compat fields, and unknown-field preservation — a lossy parse silently destroys months of learned posterior state on first cycle flush.

Budget/money safety during migration: reservation state machine must restore across the Python->Rust cutover without double-spend or free budget; terminal-reservation resurrection guard, settle-failure leaves-reservation-active direction, Boltz cooldown-not-burned-on-dry-run, and budget-blocks-don't-poison-stats are all incident-derived (v2.17.1, P4-*) invariants with real-money consequences.

cln-plugin crate surface gaps: verify it supports dynamic options with change callbacks (setconfig rejection via Err), all four subscriptions, custom notifications, and option counts of ~119 before committing; the DB-override-beats-setconfig refresh precedence caused production incidents in Python and must be rebuilt deliberately.

External-process/HTTP integration: boltzcli via tokio::process with sudo argv, version floor, timeout, and three separate error-substring detection paths; LN+ HTTPS with signmessage auth and strict input validation (pubkey regex, challenge sanitization) — an irreversible 48h funding obligation rides on this state machine surviving restarts.

Constant/ordering fidelity: many constants and clamp orderings encode named production incidents (2026-06-12 fee runaway, 2026-07-03 absorbing state, strict '>' vs '>=' gates, decay-before-check Vegas ordering, stall-multiplier-after-max P8-002) — treat every constant as load-bearing; port against the 3,091-test suite and golden fixtures rather than the prose docs.

Scope/sequencing risk: fee_controller (8.6k LOC) and capacity_planner (4.2k LOC) are dense monoliths; without dry-run shadow phases and per-loop cutover flags (already supported: dry_run, econ_governor_* gates, planner_execute_closes) a big-bang swap risks unattributable revenue regressions on a live routing node with a 125k sats/month target.
synthesis.rust_stack_recommendation: Plugin framework: official cln-plugin crate (options/subscriptions/RPC methods, stdin/stdout JSON-RPC) + cln-rpc for unix-socket calls, each call wrapped in tokio::time::timeout to replace ThreadSafeRpcProxy (preserve RPCTimeoutError/RPCOverloadedError strings; exempt wait* long-polls). Async runtime: tokio (multi-thread) with tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken replacing shutdown_event, tokio::select! loops for the 8 scheduler tasks, tokio::process for boltzcli, and single-owner actor tasks (mpsc) where Python relied on one lock spanning DB I/O (fee cycle, swap journal). SQLite: rusqlite with bundled feature OFF (link system sqlite to match WAL/pragma behavior) — per-subsystem connections behind Mutex or a dedicated writer task; explicit BEGIN IMMEDIATE via rusqlite::TransactionBehavior::Immediate for both reservation paths; busy_timeout 5000ms; wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) on shutdown. Serde: serde + serde_json for all wire/RPC shapes with #[serde(flatten)] unknown-field preservation for v2_state_json; a small hand-written canonical-JSON emitter (BTreeMap keys, no whitespace, ensure_ascii=False equivalent) for idempotency hashing — do not trust default serializer ordering; jsonschema crate in tests to validate against schemas/*.v0/v1. Numerics/RNG: hand-rolled 3x3 matrix ops transcribed from Python (not nalgebra) for DTS parity; rand + rand_distr::Normal for gauss sampling (distribution-equivalent, injected via a trait for deterministic tests); sha2 for idempotency keys/derive_seed. HTTP (LN+): reqwest with rustls-tls, form-encoded POST. Errors: thiserror for typed domains (money errors fail closed), anyhow only at loop boundaries; every RPC handler wrapped panic-safe (catch_unwind or task isolation). Testing: cargo test + insta for golden-fixture snapshots (htlcmax, close_protection, planner pairs), the existing 40-scenario conformance corpus driven through tools/conformance/validate_fixtures.py against Rust output as the cross-language gate, proptest for money-math/rounding invariants, a Python-vs-Rust RPC response diff harness over shared DB snapshots, and criterion optionally for the cycle hot paths. Misc: chrono only for display (all decision time is integer unix seconds), tracing bridged into plugin.log levels (info/warn/debug).

components.name: plugin-bootstrap-and-init
components.responsibility: The @plugin.init() handler (lines 2309-3486): registers SIGTERM handler + atexit cleanup, parses all 119 options into a Config dataclass (filtered to known fields so partial deployments don't crash), runs non-fatal CLN version-floor and boltzcli version probes plus a bookkeeper dependency check, opens the SQLite Database and loads persisted config overrides, hydrates the forwards table from paged listforwards, takes a peer-connection baseline, constructs all module objects (PolicyManager, ProfitabilityAnalyzer, FlowAnalyzer, CapacityPlanner, FeeController, EconShadow, EVRebalancer+RebalanceEngineV2, CapitalEfficiencyAnalyzer, CapexBudgetEngine, LNPlus client/lifecycle/evaluator, SegmentObservationStore, BoltzCliManager, DataService), wires data_service into each, and starts 8 daemon threads. Entry point is bare `plugin.run()` under __main__.
components.rust_considerations: cln-plugin builds options/subscriptions/methods before .start(); init logic moves into the configure/start closure. Forward hydration must preserve _compute_forward_hydration_start semantics exactly: empty table -> now - max(flow_window_days,14)*86400; non-empty -> None if gap <= FORWARD_HYDRATION_EVENT_JITTER_SECONDS, else max(last_ts-86400, now-(max(flow_window_days+1,15))*86400); paged listforwards(index=created, limit=1000, max 10000 pages) with fallback to full settled fetch on any error. Shutdown: SIGTERM sets shutdown_event then SystemExit; atexit does bounded 10s executor drain (_ATEXIT_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT), rebalance-engine pool shutdown, then DB connection close. In Rust: tokio signal handling + CancellationToken; the bounded-drain hack disappears but the 'never block exit forever on a wedged lightningd' property must be kept.

components.name: threadsafe-rpc-proxy
components.responsibility: Lines ~686-1075: ThreadSafeRpcProxy wraps pyln's per-call unix-socket RPC in a ThreadPoolExecutor(16 workers, plus a 4-worker async pool for fire_and_forget), giving every call a caller-side timeout (RPCTimeoutError), pool-saturation detection (RPCOverloadedError), and a monkeypatched socket-level recv timeout (proxy timeout + 5s buffer) that frees wedged workers; wait* long-poll methods are exempted. ThreadSafePluginProxy wraps plugin.log for thread use. shutdown_event (threading.Event) is the global cancellation primitive.
components.rust_considerations: This whole component exists because pyln RPC blocks in recv() with no timeout and is not thread-safe. In Rust use cln-rpc with one connection per call (or a pool) wrapped in tokio::time::timeout — the UnixSocket.connect monkeypatch has no equivalent and none is needed. Preserve the error taxonomy and strings callers/loops match on: RPCTimeoutError ("RPC timeout for method: {method}"), RPCBreakerOpen (subclass kept for back-compat, loops catch it alongside timeout and skip the cycle with a warn log), RPCOverloadedError ("RPC worker pool saturated for method: {method}"). Loops treat these as degraded-skip, all other exceptions as error-log-and-continue. Keep the wait*/waitsendpay/waitblockheight long-poll exemption (no timeout).

components.name: option-registration-and-config
components.responsibility: 119 plugin.add_option registrations (lines 1076-1900), all string-typed and hand-parsed in init via _safe_int/_safe_float helpers that raise descriptive ValueError ("Invalid integer for config '{key}': {value!r}"). Many are dynamic=True (settable via setconfig at runtime). revenue-config get/set persists overrides to the DB and _refresh_dynamic_config re-applies them inside loops before each cycle. Enum-valued options are validated with warn-and-use-default rather than failure.
components.rust_considerations: Full option list (prefix revenue-ops-): db-path; flow-interval; fee-interval; rebalance-interval; min-fee-ppm; min-fee-ppm-saturated; max-fee-ppm; fee-profile; fee-market-boundary-{enabled,min-competitors,margin-ppm,margin-ratio,max-downshift-ratio,cache-seconds}; market-fee-mode; base-fee-policy; neighbor-median-min-competitors; rebalance-min-profit; rebalance-emergency-local-ratio; rebalance-drift-override-ratio; rebalance-hold-margin; pair-fee-cap-ppm; rebalance-activity-{window-seconds,penalty-coeff,penalty-cap-frac}; rebalance-utilization-{window-days,floor,ceiling,min-forwards}; rebalance-size-{tiered-targets,reference-percentile}; rebalance-small-channel-band-half-width; flow-window-days; daily-budget-sats; growth-budget-{enabled,earned-fraction,experiment-fraction,max-extra-sats,hard-ceiling-sats}; diagnostic-rebalance-max-fee-sats; allow-zero-cost-auto-rebalance-when-budget-zero; weekly-budget-sats; min-wallet-reserve; dry-run; htlc-congestion-threshold; enable-reputation; reputation-decay; enable-kelly; vegas-reflex; vegas-decay; rpc-timeout-seconds; reservation-timeout-hours; hot-channel-protection-{enabled,override-peers,min-velocity,min-marginal-roi,profit-budget-pct,max-chunk-multiplier,min-cooldown-hours}; boltz-{enabled,cli-path,datadir,use-sudo,sudo-user,timeout-seconds,daily-budget-sats,enforce-budget,btc-wallet,lbtc-wallet,routing-fee-limit-ppm,max-withdraw-sats}; boltz-auto-cycle-{enabled,interval-minutes,max-actions,startup-delay-seconds}; receivable-ratio-{target,floor}; boltz-structural-budget-sats; drain-fee-discount-max; node-drain-bias-{enabled,max}; enable-dynamic-htlcmax; htlcmax-{source,sink,balanced}-pct; expansion-treasury-{enabled,onchain-target-sats,min-deficit-sats,preferred-currency,max-actions,min-source-local-pct,exclude-protected}; planner-{enabled,interval,dry-run,max-opens-per-cycle,max-closes-per-cycle,close-fee-reserve-multiplier,close-fee-cap-sats,close-feerange-enabled,min-channel-sats,max-channel-sats,max-fee-rate,min-annual-roi-pct,execute-closes}; lnplus-{swaps-enabled,execute-applications,swap-preference-margin,max-duration-months,min-peer-positive-ratings,min-peer-rank,max-participants,min-participants,apply-feerate-ceiling,pending-timeout-days,inbound-credit-factor,watcher-interval}; rebalance-router; askrene-layers. Booleans parse 'true'/'1'/'yes' variants — keep string tolerance. cln-plugin supports typed + dynamic options natively; the DB-persisted revenue-config override layer (config versioning, snapshot() for mid-loop consistency) must be reimplemented — loops always read a config snapshot so a mid-cycle change can't tear values.

components.name: background-scheduler-loops
components.responsibility: Eight daemon threads started at end of init, each with a canonical guard wrapping the entire iteration (work + sleep tail) so no exception kills the thread, a heartbeat record for revenue-health liveness reporting, interruptible sleep via shutdown_event.wait(), and 30s backoff (_LOOP_BACKOFF_SECONDS) on unhandled errors.
components.rust_considerations: Loops and timings a port must preserve: flow-analysis (start delay 30s; interval max(60, flow_interval) with ±20% integer jitter; also runs DB cleanup_old_data(max(8, flow_window_days+1) days) and expired-policy cleanup each pass), fee-adjustment (delay 90s; max(60, fee_interval) ±20%), rebalance-check (delay 180s; max(60, rebalance_interval) ±20%), startup-snapshot (one-shot after 60s, closes its thread-local DB conn on exit), financial-snapshot (delay 300s, immediate initial snapshot + closure-resolution reconcile, then every 86400s ±10%, sleep-tail at TOP of loop), boltz-auto-cycle (returns immediately if boltz disabled; startup delay boltz_auto_cycle_startup_delay_seconds default 120; interval boltz_auto_cycle_interval_minutes*60 default 15m, ±10% jitter; publishes state via _boltz_auto_cycle_mark_state incl. next_run_ts), capacity-planner (only if planner_enabled; startup delay = remaining interval since last DB-recorded cycle else 300s warmup; interval max(600, planner_interval) ±20%), lnplus-watcher (delay 180s; lnplus_watcher_interval clamped to bounds, default 3600s, ±10%). Intervals are re-read from a config snapshot every iteration (dynamic reconfig). Map to tokio tasks with tokio::select! on a CancellationToken; jitter uses inclusive randint(-j, j).

components.name: core-cycle-functions
components.responsibility: run_flow_analysis / run_fee_adjustment / run_rebalance_check plus helpers (_snapshot_peers_once, _take_financial_snapshot, _reconcile_closure_resolutions, _refresh_dynamic_config, _run_boltz_auto_cycle_once, budget-limit and liquidity-cost provider closures injected into RebalanceEngine). These orchestrate the module objects: flow analysis marks channels Source/Sink/Balanced and applies reputation decay; fee adjustment drives FeeController per channel; rebalance check drives EVRebalancer/RebalanceEngineV2.
components.rust_considerations: financial snapshot converts msat->sats with integer floor division (revenue_msat // 1000) — keep integer math, no floats, for all sat/msat conversions. Budget provider closures (_total_cost_budget_limit_provider, _non_rebalance_liquidity_cost_components) are dependency-injection seams the RebalanceEngine calls back into — in Rust these become trait objects or channels; the unified total-cost budget semantics (rebalance + boltz + capex share one ceiling) must be identical.

components.name: rpc-method-surface-core
components.responsibility: 36 operator-facing @plugin.method handlers (lines 3667-6350). Read/debug: revenue-status (plugin+loop status), revenue-health (health incl. loop-liveness heartbeats), revenue-fee-debug (per-channel fee decision inputs), revenue-rebalance-debug (rebalance scoring diagnostics), revenue-analyze (on-demand flow analysis, optional single channel), revenue-profitability (per-channel/node P&L, refresh flag), revenue-history (lifetime stats), revenue-report (summary/detailed earnings report), revenue-dashboard (consolidated financial dashboard over window_days), revenue-capacity-report, revenue-planner-status/-candidates/-candidate-sources/-history, revenue-lnplus-status, revenue-econ-snapshot, revenue-list-ignored, revenue-list-banned, revenue-profile-preview (fee-profile what-if). Actions: revenue-rebalance-cycle (run engine cycle now, max_candidates=20), revenue-fee-cycle, revenue-wake-all (kick all loops), revenue-planner-execute, revenue-set-fee (manual fee with force override), revenue-rebalance (manual pair rebalance), revenue-ignore/-unignore, revenue-ban/-unban, revenue-policy (per-peer fee/rebalance policy get/set/list), revenue-hot-channel-protection-peers (override-list management), revenue-config (runtime get/set persisted overrides), revenue-econ-reconcile/-econ-cycle (EconShadow governor), revenue-cleanup-closed, revenue-clear-reservations, revenue-lnplus-breaker-clear/-abandon/-backfill.
components.rust_considerations: Response dict shapes are consumed by lightning-cli users, cl-hive, and scripts/ tooling — field names and nesting must be preserved verbatim (serde structs with exact key names). pyln maps Python kwargs with defaults to optional JSON-RPC params by name or position; cln-plugin methods must accept both named and positional params with the same defaults (e.g. revenue-report(report_type="summary"), revenue-dashboard(window_days=30), revenue-rebalance-cycle(max_candidates=20)). Errors are raised as RpcError with specific message strings; every handler has a top-level guard so a bug returns a JSON-RPC error rather than killing the plugin — Rust handlers must be panic-safe.

components.name: notification-subscriptions
components.responsibility: Four @plugin.subscribe handlers (lines 6661-7500), each wrapped in a top-level try/except so an exception can never crash CLN event processing. forward_event: records settled forwards + peer reputation (success/fail), preferring a single-transaction combined write (record_forward_and_reputation) over two autocommit writes; failed forwards penalize the in_channel peer only on downstream failure, never local_failed. connect/disconnect: peer uptime tracking snapshots. channel_state_changed: detects closures (ONCHAIN, CLOSED, FUNDING_SPEND_SEEN), queries bookkeeper for actual on-chain fees, records closure costs, archives channel P&L.
components.rust_considerations: Handler-and-hydration overlap is deliberately safe via a unique-index dedup on the forwards table — the Rust port must keep INSERT OR IGNORE semantics so startup hydration and live forward_event inserts can race. SCID normalization (normalize_scid) and in_channel->peer resolution caching matter for correctness. Subscriptions run on the plugin's event path: keep them fast and non-blocking (offload DB writes if needed) and infallible (no panics propagate).

components.name: spend-budget-and-capex-rpcs
components.responsibility: Budget/ledger RPC surface (lines 7510-7742): revenue-total-cost-budget (unified spend budget status over window_hours), revenue-capex-status (CapexBudgetEngine state), and the spend ledger CRUD: revenue-spend-ledger (list), revenue-spend-reserve (create reservation), revenue-spend-release (release by id), revenue-spend-release-stale (sweep expired), revenue-spend-settle (settle actual cost).
components.rust_considerations: Reservation lifecycle semantics are load-bearing: reservations time out after revenue-ops-reservation-timeout-hours, stale ones are released at startup and on demand, and the daily/weekly budget checks count active reservations plus settled spend. Growth-budget extension (earned-fraction/experiment-fraction/max-extra/hard-ceiling) layers on top of daily-budget-sats. Any drift here changes real money spent.

components.name: boltz-swap-rpcs-and-auto-cycle
components.responsibility: Boltz surface (lines 7743-10378): pass-through/managed swap RPCs — revenue-boltz-quote, -loop-out, -loop-in, -status, -history, -external-pay-ignores, -budget, -wallet, -refund, -claim, -chainswap, -withdraw, -deposit, -backup, -backup-verify — plus the automation layer: revenue-boltz-balance-recommendations (receivable-ratio-driven loop suggestions), revenue-boltz-auto-cycle-status / -run-now, revenue-boltz-balance-cycle (one profit-gated balance pass), and the expansion treasury trio revenue-boltz-expansion-treasury-status / -recommendations / -cycle (accumulate on-chain funds toward a channel-open target). _run_boltz_auto_cycle_once is shared by the scheduler loop and run-now.
components.rust_considerations: BoltzCliManager shells out to an external boltzcli binary (optionally via sudo -u <user>) with a version floor check and timeout — in Rust use tokio::process with the same argv construction, sudo path, and timeout; embedded-error detection in loop_in/chainswap output parsing was a v2.17.1 bug fix and must be ported. Auto-cycle state (_boltz_auto_cycle_mark_state: enabled/thread_started/next_run_ts/last_result) feeds revenue-boltz-auto-cycle-status — keep field names. Cooldown must NOT be burned on dry-run or policy-block (v2.17.1 fix). Boltz spend is gated by its own daily budget (boltz-daily-budget-sats, enforce-budget) AND the unified total-cost budget.

lens_maps.lens: CLN plugin entrypoint (cl-revenue-ops.py, 10,378 LOC): RPC surface, options, subscriptions, threading/lifecycle — Rust port prep
external_dependencies: pyln-client (Plugin framework, JSON-RPC over unix socket, UnixSocket internals monkeypatched for recv timeouts)
CLN bookkeeper plugin (bkpr-* RPCs for on-chain cost attribution; detected at init, degraded mode if absent)

CLN RPCs: listforwards (paged index=created), listpeers/listpeerchannels, listchannels, setchannel, getinfo, listfunds, sendpay/waitsendpay, askrene getroutes + layers (native rebalance routing), fundchannel/close (planner), bkpr-listaccountevents/bkpr-listincome, wait* long-poll methods (timeout-exempt)
SQLite (modules/database, thread-local connections, migrations, unique-index forward dedup)
boltzcli external binary via subprocess (optional sudo -u, version floor, timeout)
LN+ (lightningnetwork.plus) HTTP API via LNPlusClient with circuit breaker
Python stdlib: threading (8 daemon loops + Event), concurrent.futures ThreadPoolExecutor (16+4 RPC workers), signal/atexit, random (interval jitter)
lens_maps.port_notes: STARTUP ORDER (must be preserved): register SIGTERM+atexit first (handlers None-guard uninitialized globals) -> parse options with _safe_int/_safe_float (error string format \"Invalid integer for config '{key}': {value!r}\") -> Config(**filtered kwargs) dropping unknown fields with a warning (partial-deployment tolerance) -> install RPC socket-timeout guard -> ThreadSafePluginProxy -> DataService -> non-fatal CLN/boltzcli version probes + bookkeeper check -> Database(db_path) + release stale budget reservations + load DB config overrides -> bounded forwards hydration -> peer baseline snapshot -> construct modules in order (PolicyManager, Profitability, FlowAnalyzer, CapacityPlanner, FeeController, EconShadow, EVRebalancer/RebalanceEngineV2, CapitalEfficiency, CapexBudgetEngine, LNPlus, SegmentObservationStore, BoltzCliManager) -> wire data_service everywhere -> spawn 8 loops -> log \"cl-revenue-ops plugin initialized successfully!\". SHUTDOWN: SIGTERM -> shutdown_event.set() + SystemExit(0); atexit sets the event again (covers stdin-EOF exit where SIGTERM never fires), drains RPC executors bounded at 10s, shuts rebalance engine pool, closes DB connections. Every loop sleep is shutdown_event.wait(t) — interruption must be immediate, never sleep-until-timer. LOOP CONTRACT: guard wraps the ENTIRE iteration including the sleep tail; RPCTimeoutError/RPCBreakerOpen = warn + skip cycle; any other error = error log + continue; unhandled iteration error = 30s backoff. Heartbeats (_record_loop_heartbeat with name + interval, one_shot for startup-snapshot) feed revenue-health loop-liveness — keep names: flow-analysis, fee-adjustment, rebalance-check, startup-snapshot, financial-snapshot, boltz-auto-cycle, capacity-planner, lnplus-watcher. BUDGET SEMANTICS: one unified total-cost ceiling spans rebalance fees, Boltz swap costs, and capex; daily-budget-sats + optional growth-budget extension (earned/experiment fractions, max-extra, hard ceiling) + weekly-budget-sats; reservations expire after reservation-timeout-hours and are swept at startup; allow-zero-cost-auto-rebalance-when-budget-zero is a distinct code path; budget blocks must NOT poison futility/success-rate stats (v2.17.1 fix); Boltz cooldown must not burn on dry-run/policy-block. MONEY MATH: all sat/msat conversions are integer floor division; jitter is inclusive randint(-j,+j) on integer seconds. RPC RESPONSE SHAPES: dict field names are a public contract (CLI + scripts + tests assert them); methods take named-or-positional optional params with Python-default parity. DYNAMIC CONFIG: many options dynamic=True and revenue-config persists overrides to DB; loops read config.snapshot() each iteration so mid-cycle mutation never tears — Rust needs an Arc<RwLock<Config>> snapshot-clone equivalent plus the DB override load/apply (_refresh_dynamic_config) before planner/lnplus/boltz cycles. SUBSCRIPTION SAFETY: handlers are infallible (top-level catch); forward inserts dedup via unique index so hydration and live events can race; failed forwards penalize in_channel peer only on downstream failure, never local_failed. The 3091-test suite in tests/ encodes most of these invariants — port against it."

components.name: GaussianThompsonState (DTS market pricing)
components.responsibility: Per-channel Discounted Gaussian Thompson Sampling posterior over the fee-revenue relationship. Primary sampler is a Bayesian quadratic regression R(F)=a*F^2+b*F+c on basis phi(F)=[F^2,F,1] with a hand-rolled 3x3 precision matrix (Sarrus determinant, explicit inverse, Cholesky factorization for correlated coefficient sampling); a legacy Normal-Normal conjugate path remains for sparse data. Layers on top: contextual posteriors keyed by time-bucket/balance-class (bounded advisory offset, CTX_OFFSET_CAP_FRAC=0.20), durable posterior_bias nudges (time-decayed, deduped at 5% tolerance, max 50), zero-revenue streak tracking with injected downward pseudo-probes (ZERO_PROBE_STEP_FRAC=0.9, floor frac 0.3, regime override at 24 windows with 24h anchor half-life), trickle guard (TRICKLE_RESET_FRAC=0.10 of positive-rate EMA), supported-fee ceiling from the 0.90 quantile of recency-weighted positive revenue mass x1.25 headroom with 2.0x floor-escape band, and 24h-gated upward probes (stretch 1.25, min std 60).
components.rust_considerations: Pure float math with exact incident-driven constants (MIN_STD=10, REL_MIN_STD_FRAC=0.04, DECAY_HOURS=168, MAX_OBSERVATIONS=200); Cholesky/inverse fallbacks on non-PD matrices must degrade identically. Uses random.gauss — Rust needs an equivalent normal sampler; determinism differences are acceptable but distributions must match. to_dict/from_dict must round-trip legacy JSON blobs: 5-tuple and 6-tuple observations (6th element flags 'zero_probe'/'congestion'), 3-tuple legacy contextual posteriors, retained compat fields exploration_boost and reseeded_at, and ZERO_REVENUE_WEIGHT_FACTOR=0.15 rescaling applied only on legacy load.

components.name: PIDState (inventory bias)
components.responsibility: PI controller (D removed) producing a bounded 0.5x-2.0x multiplier applied to the DTS fee sample. Error = target_ratio - outbound_ratio with EWMA smoothing (alpha=0.3); target ratio by flow state (source=0.7, sink=0.3, balanced=0.5 via _PID_TARGET_RATIOS). Gains kp=2.0/ki=0.1 scaled by 1/log2(capacity_sats/1M + 2); integral accumulates in hours of wall time, clamped to +/-3.0; output mapped exponentially as multiplier = clamp(1.5^(P+I), 0.5, 2.0).
components.rust_considerations: Wall-clock dt in hours (first update dt=0); NaN/Inf ratio guard falls back to target. Serialized per channel inside v2_state_json (kp/ki/kd/ewma_error/integral_error/prev_ewma_error/last_update_time/integral_clamp) — defaults on missing keys must match from_dict.

components.name: VegasReflexState (mempool spike floor)
components.responsibility: Node-global anti-arbitrage floor raiser keyed on mempool spike ratio (current sat/vB vs 24h moving average from DB mempool_fee_history). Intensity in [0,1] decays 0.85/cycle BEFORE spike check; >=4x spike sets intensity=1.0 immediately; 2x-4x spikes trigger probabilistically (2 consecutive spikes OR random < boost*0.5, adding boost*0.3). Floor multiplier = 1 + sqrt(intensity)*2 (1.0x-3.0x). Also drives edge-triggered fleet-wide wake of sleeping channels at intensity>=0.5, re-armed below 0.3.
components.rust_considerations: Uses random.random() for probabilistic trigger. Intensity is in-memory only (not persisted); decay rate configurable via vegas_decay_rate. Decay-before-check ordering is a documented invariant.

components.name: FeeController cycle orchestrator (_adjust_all_fees / _adjust_channel_fee)
components.responsibility: The core control loop: for each channel compute bounded_target = clamp(DTS_sample x PID_multiplier, max(effective_min_fee, chain-cost floor, rebalance floor, vegas floor), flow-adjusted ceiling), then blended_target = current + blend_ratio*(bounded - current), then damp by per-cycle delta cap, then gossip-gate hysteresis. Contains the fee priority chain (congestion > bounded low-fee exploration > DTS+PID), deadband sleep/wake hysteresis (3 stable cycles to sleep, 20% revenue spike wakes), congestion episodes (per-cycle cap min(ceiling, max(2x, +250ppm)), episode cap 4x entry fee, 2 quiet cycles to exit, undamped step only on episode entry edge), zero-flow ratchet guard (raise freeze at streak 8, forced 0.85x downshift at streak 24 rate-limited to every 12 cycles, soft floor at 0.5x earning anchor, streaks cadence-scaled by meaningful-gap EMA capped 168h), Kalman demand divisor clamp(ed/0.5, 1.0, 2.0) normalizing revenue observations, unroutable-window censoring (spendable < 25k sats excluded from posterior), and gossip refresh nudges (+1 ppm) for frozen channels after 24h idle/broadcast/cooldown.
components.rust_considerations: Single RLock (_state_lock) held across DB I/O for the whole cycle is a documented architectural constraint (I-14) — in Rust prefer a single-owner task but preserve the atomicity guarantees hook threads rely on (record_failed_forward mutates Thompson state under the same lock, with lazy DB seeding of evicted state). FeeProfileSettings 'active' vs 'conservative' constant tables must be byte-identical. Gossip gate: 5% suppression band persists suppressed target as cycle.pending_target_ppm and blends FROM it next cycle; anchor cleared when a new sample crosses it — deliberate deadband semantics.

components.name: Market intelligence (neighbor gossip)
components.responsibility: Gossip-derived competitive signals: weighted median of neighbor fees to the same peer (weight = capacity_millions x 1/age_days, fee range filter 1-10000 ppm, CLN-default tuple 1000msat/10ppm excluded, >=3 competitors required, 30-min cache with 500-entry eviction), percentile variant, capacity-rank-based undercut percent (5%-15% base by rank, +5% if median>300, halved if median<100, clamped [0.03, 0.20], invert_rank for premium mode), variance-gated undercut (only when posterior std <= max(100, 0.04*fee), strict '>' comparison required; skipped when outbound_ratio < 0.35), initial-fee prior selection and seeding (INITIAL_PRIOR_NUDGE_WEIGHT=0.3). _get_market_boundary_fee is a deprecated stub that must ALWAYS return None even when fee_market_boundary_enabled=true is persisted.
components.rust_considerations: Neighbor cache is written outside _state_lock by design (pre-lock prefetch) — snapshot-iterate on eviction. Cycle-scoped frozen-observation memoization (_frozen_observation) ensures every channel in one cycle sees the same gossip values. listchannels per-peer calls are the expensive RPC; TTL from _gossip_cache_ttl_seconds.

components.name: Floors and safety rails
components.responsibility: The hard-safety concern. _calculate_floor: max(amortized replacement chain cost over lifetime volume, congestion risk premium from live sat/vB x 150 vbytes x 0.001 force-close probability over 50k-sat reference HTLC) x 1.2 stall multiplier for high-latency peers (avg>10s or std>5s) — multiplier applied AFTER the max (P8-002). Rebalance cost floor (refill cost x 1.20 margin, >=4 samples in 30-day dest-only rebalance_costs window; sink/dormant exempt; cost_ppm capped 5000). Class-aware min fee: saturated (outbound>=0.85) or source channels get min(min_fee_ppm, min_fee_ppm_saturated) unless the channel is a flow-balanced router (7d |out-in|/(out+in) <= 0.33 and turnover >= 0.25x capacity). Congestion detection prefers live outgoing-HTLC utilization over the flow snapshot, ignoring snapshot labels staler than 2x flow_interval.
components.rust_considerations: Floor composition order is load-bearing: effective_min_fee_ppm replaces ONLY the min_fee_ppm term; chain-cost floor, rebalance floor, and vegas multiplier still compose via max() on top. ChainCostDefaults constants live in config.py. Per-cycle peer-latency memoization (parallel channels to one peer share one query).

components.name: Admission policy (htlcmax valve)
components.responsibility: Dynamic htlc_max advertisement: htlc_max_msat = clamp(min(flow-class pct of capacity [htlcmax_source_pct/sink/balanced], spendable_msat x 0.85), 10_000_000 msat, capacity_msat); returns None when enable_dynamic_htlcmax is off (string 'true'/'1'/'yes' or bool True only). Churn deadband: a standalone htlcmax delta forces a broadcast only if |delta| > 10% of currently advertised value (current<=0 always broadcasts; equal never does); it always piggybacks on broadcasts happening anyway.
components.rust_considerations: Pure functions of (cfg, channel_info, flow_state) — no RPC/DB/clock. Pinned by golden fixtures (tests/golden/fixtures/htlcmax/); the port must reproduce these byte-identically, including int truncation of capacity*pct and spendable*0.85.

components.name: Drain bias helpers
components.responsibility: Node-liquidity-aware drain discount. Pure functions: compute_node_receivable_ratio over CHANNELD_NORMAL channels (returns 1.0 on no capacity), node_drain_pressure linear ramp between floor and target (degenerate target<=floor guard), effective_drain_discount_max = max(static drain_fee_discount_max, node_drain_bias_max x pressure) but ONLY when node_drain_bias_enabled is an actual bool True (default-off invariant). _drain_fee_multiplier: bounded linear discount for channels above the high-liquidity threshold with zero forwards in the window; 1.0 no-op otherwise; a bias, not an override — min-fee rails still clamp downstream.
components.rust_considerations: Never double-counts per-peer drain_direction hints (node-aggregate only, documented invariant). The _cfg_bool/_cfg_float MagicMock defenses are Python-test artifacts; in Rust the invariant to keep is 'feature must default off and static operator discount is never reduced'.

components.name: Per-channel state persistence
components.responsibility: ChannelFeeState (thompson + pid + revenue/broadcast tracking + sleep hysteresis) and ChannelCycleState (trend/step legacy fields, congestion episode tracker, pending_target_ppm, gossip refresh) merged into one fee_strategy_state row per channel: scalar columns plus a v2_state_json blob holding thompson/pid/cycle payloads. Shared fields (last_gossip_refresh, last_broadcast_at, dynamic_htlcmin_baseline_msat) use __setattr__ tracking so only explicitly-assigned values win the merge between the two state objects. During a cycle, rows are enqueued last-write-wins and flushed once via update_fee_strategy_states_batch (falling back to per-row writes on error); out-of-cycle saves (manual RPC, hooks, set_initial_fee) write immediately.
components.rust_considerations: The v2_state_json blob (~50KB/channel) is serialized exactly once at flush — a deliberate perf contract. from_v2_dict must accept legacy layouts: top-level 'thompson_state', algorithm_version values 'thompson_aimd_v1' and 'dts_pid_v1', nested 'fee_state'/'cycle_state' dicts. Enqueued rows alias live structures; safe only because flush runs in the same lock scope as the loop — a Rust port must either clone or preserve that exclusivity.

components.name: Fee execution (set_channel_fee)
components.responsibility: Last-line-of-defense execution layer: absolute clamp [0, 100000] ppm always; economic clamp [econ_min_fee, max_fee_ppm] unless enforce_limits=False; a cycle-provided effective_min_fee_ppm may only LOWER the min term (never raise, never exceed global min_fee_ppm). Calls CLN setchannel with fee_ppm, base fee (base_fee_msat, default 0), and optional htlcmin/htlcmax; extracts effective values from the setchannel response; records fee_changes audit rows; handles policy-change hooks (PASSIVE skips, STATIC pins). Returns {success, channel_id, fee_ppm, message, ...} dict consumed by RPC commands.
components.rust_considerations: Result dict shape and clamp log format ('FEE_LIMIT: Clamped fee for {id[:16]}... from X to Y (limits: A-B PPM)') are operator-visible surfaces. setchannel htlc bound extraction tolerates multiple CLN response field spellings (_extract_local_htlc_bounds / _extract_setchannel_effective_values). ABS bounds are intentionally independent of configured min/max.

components.name: Governor authorization
components.responsibility: When econ_governor_fees_enabled is True, every automated fee broadcast is authorized through GovernorFacade with a zero-cost SET_FEE intent (reserve_spend stubbed to always-true since a fee change is reversible; gates are paused-state, authority_allows(authority_level, 'fees'), and staleness). Appends intent_proposed events with the rendered explanation and optional canonical_snapshot_id to the econ ledger. FAILS CLOSED: any exception returns (False, 'internal_error ({e})').
components.rust_considerations: Fail-closed semantics and the 'internal_error ({e})' reason string are contract. Intent envelope fields (expires_at=now+600, priority=50, budget_bucket='fees', origin_policy='fee_controller_governed', reversible=true, idempotency_key hashing snapshot_id 'fee-broadcast-{now}') must match the econ ledger's expectations shared with other governed subsystems.

components.name: Failed-forward signal
components.responsibility: Hook-thread path converting fee-relevant forward failures (WIRE_FEE_INSUFFICIENT family only; failures with no usable failcode/failreason are dropped) into a durable posterior nudge at 0.8x current fee on the OUTGOING channel. Weight = 0.1 x min(3.0, 1 + log10(amount_sats)/3). Suppressed within 3600s of our own fee change (gossip-settle) and rate-limited to one nudge per 1800s per channel. Lazily seeds evicted fee state from the persisted row but never fabricates fresh state from a failure.
components.rust_considerations: Runs on the forward_event hook thread concurrently with the fee loop; shares _state_lock. Never a channel's first posterior evidence — the persisted-DTS check inspects the RAW v2 row, not the defaulted payload.

components.name: Config
components.responsibility: Typed option registry with per-key validation ranges and enum sets, snapshot() for consistent per-cycle reads, and ChainCostDefaults/LiquidityBuckets constants. Fee-relevant knobs include min/max_fee_ppm (min range 5-100000), min_fee_ppm_saturated (0-1000, must be < min_fee_ppm to apply), fee_profile ('active'|'conservative'), fee_interval, drain_fee_discount_max (0-0.5), node_drain_bias_enabled/max, enable_dynamic_htlcmax + htlcmax_{source,sink,balanced}_pct, htlc_congestion_threshold, enable_vegas_reflex + vegas_decay_rate, thompson_prior_std_fee (10-500), base_fee_msat (0-10000), econ_governor_fees_enabled, market_fee_mode ('undercut'|'match'|'premium'|'competition_aware'), and the retired fee_market_boundary_* family kept for compatibility.
components.rust_considerations: Config overrides persist in the config_overrides DB table and are surfaced via revenue-config get/set RPC — the port must keep key names, validation ranges, and clamp behavior identical or persisted operator overrides break. snapshot() semantics (immutable view per cycle) matter for consistency.

components.name: Database persistence (fee-relevant subset)
components.responsibility: SQLite layer. Fee-stack tables: fee_strategy_state (PK channel_id, scalar columns + v2_state_json added by additive idempotent migrations), fee_changes audit log, mempool_fee_history (record_mempool_fee/get_mempool_ma 24h MA for Vegas), rebalance_costs + get_channel_cost_history (dest-only refill costs for the floor), forwards/daily stats and get_all_channel_flow_windows (7d directional flow for the balanced-router exemption), channel_states (flow state snapshots feeding congestion fallback), peer latency stats (stall defense), peer_policies.
components.rust_considerations: Migrations are additive CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + try/except ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN — a Rust port must read/write existing production DBs in place. update_fee_strategy_states_batch is the one-transaction cycle flush. Python sqlite3 is effectively serialized; Rust (rusqlite) must handle multi-thread access (hook threads + cycle) with equivalent consistency.

components.name: DataService (RPC cache)
components.responsibility: Unified CLN RPC access with tiered TTL caching: FOREVER (node_id, network, configs), LONG 300s (listnodes, feerates), MEDIUM 30s (listpeerchannels, listfunds, listpeers), NEVER for transactional calls (setchannel, sendpay). 256-entry LRU-by-timestamp cap with stale-on-read eviction; thread-safe via locks. The fee stack uses get_peer_channels, get_channels (gossip listchannels per peer), get_feerates (Vegas + chain costs), set_channel.
components.rust_considerations: TTL tiers and the setchannel-never-cached rule are correctness-relevant (stale channel lists cause fee writes to dead channels). In Rust map to cln-plugin async RPC; the cache's cross-module sharing pattern implies Arc<Mutex<...>> or a single-owner actor.

components.name: PolicyManager
components.responsibility: Per-peer operator policy overrides consumed by the fee cycle: FeeStrategy enum DYNAMIC (default, DTS+PID) / STATIC (pinned fee_ppm_target) / PASSIVE (never touch), plus fee_multiplier_min/max overrides, tags, and expiring policies (expires_at auto-revert). PASSIVE/STATIC map to FeeReasonCode.POLICY_PASSIVE/POLICY_STATIC skip/override paths in the cycle.
components.rust_considerations: Only the fee-relevant slice (~1377 LOC total file, much is rebalance policy). Enum string values ('dynamic'/'static'/'passive') are persisted in peer_policies and exposed over RPC — must not change.

external_dependencies: CLN RPC: setchannel (fee_base, fee_ppm, htlcmin/htlcmax)
CLN RPC: listpeerchannels (balances, HTLC in-flight, spendable_msat, max_accepted_htlcs)
CLN RPC: listchannels (per-peer gossip for neighbor median/percentile/undercut)
CLN RPC: getinfo (node id)
CLN RPC: feerates (Vegas Reflex sat/vB + dynamic chain costs)
CLN RPC: listnodes
CLN forward_event hook (failed-forward nudges)
pyln.client (Plugin, RpcError) -> cln-plugin crate in Rust
sqlite3 stdlib -> rusqlite
Python stdlib: random (gauss, random), math, json, threading
lens_maps.port_notes: Core control law to preserve exactly: bounded_target = clamp(DTS_sample x PID_mult, max(effective_min_fee, chain_floor, rebalance_floor x vegas_mult composition via max()), flow_adjusted_ceiling); blended = current + blend_ratio x (bounded - current); final = damp(blended, per-cycle delta cap = max(ratio x current, min_delta_ppm)); then 5% gossip-gate with pending_target_ppm carry-forward (targets inside the band are absorbed BY DESIGN; the pending anchor clears when a sample crosses it). Fee priority chain: congestion > bounded low-fee exploration > DTS+PID. Named algorithms a port must reimplement: Bayesian quadratic regression Thompson sampling (3x3 precision matrix, Cholesky draw, discount gamma 0.98 normal / 0.992 sparse), Normal-Normal conjugate fallback, PI controller with EWMA(0.3) error, 1/log2(cap/1M+2) gain scaling and 1.5^u exponential output clamp [0.5,2.0], Vegas exponential-decay intensity (decay BEFORE spike check; sqrt curve 1x-3x), Kalman demand divisor clamp(ed/0.5, 1.0, 2.0) (may at most HALVE an observation, never amplify), capacity-x-recency weighted median for neighbor fees, and the zero-flow ratchet (freeze at streak 8, 0.85x step every 12 cycles at streak 24, soft floor 0.5x earning anchor, cadence-scaled thresholds). Behavioral contracts: (1) _get_market_boundary_fee must return None unconditionally even with persisted fee_market_boundary_enabled=true; (2) governed broadcast fails closed with reason string 'internal_error ({e})' and a zero-cost SET_FEE intent (expires now+600, priority 50, budget_bucket 'fees', reversible); (3) set_channel_fee returns {success, channel_id, fee_ppm, message} with ABS clamp [0,100000] always and economic clamp unless enforce_limits=False, and effective_min_fee_ppm may only lower (never raise) the min term; (4) exploration never goes below max(floor_ppm, config floor) — near-floor channels may sit AT the floor; (5) unroutable windows (spendable < 25,000 sats) are censored data: excluded from posterior AND zero-streak; (6) zero-revenue trickle windows (< 10% of positive-rate EMA) must not reset the zero streak; (7) failure nudges only for WIRE_FEE_INSUFFICIENT, outgoing channel only, suppressed 3600s after our own fee change and rate-limited 1800s; (8) posterior nudge dedup at 5% tolerance refreshes instead of appending; (9) exploration gate comparisons use STRICT '>' against max(100, 0.04 x fee) — '>=' recreates an absorbing state; (10) undercut clamp disabled below outbound_ratio 0.35; (11) node drain bias default-off invariant: with node_drain_bias_enabled false the result is byte-identical to static drain_fee_discount_max; (12) htlcmax valve semantics are pinned by golden fixtures in tests/golden/fixtures/htlcmax/ (clamp(min(class_pct x cap, spendable x 0.85), 10k sats, cap), 10% deadband) and must stay byte-identical; (13) fee_strategy_state rows must round-trip existing production blobs including legacy thompson_aimd_v1 layouts, 5-/6-tuple observations, and compat fields exploration_boost/reseeded_at; batched cycle persistence is last-write-wins flushed once per cycle; (14) congestion: undamped jump only on episode entry edge, per-cycle cap min(ceiling, max(2x current, current+250)), whole episode capped at 4x entry fee, 2 quiet cycles to exit, and the window's observation ALWAYS reaches the posterior before the congestion target applies; (15) Vegas wake is edge-triggered once per crossing (>=0.5, re-arm <0.3), never once per cycle. Many constants encode named production incidents (2026-06-12 LOOP 101->2612ppm runaway, 2026-07-03 nexus-01 floor-pinning/absorbing-state) — treat every constant and clamp ordering as load-bearing, not tunable.
lens_maps.lens: Fee control stack (DTS+PID fee controller and its safety rails) of cl_revenue_ops, mapped for a Rust port

components.name: EVRebalancer facade + JobManager stub
components.responsibility: Top-level 'strategist': the timer-driven auto cycle entry (find_rebalance_candidates, which ALWAYS returns [] and delegates selection+execution to engine.run_cycle), capital controls (wallet reserve fail-open on RpcError, daily/weekly budget fail-closed), inbound-fee estimation (historical-first: high=median, medium=0.7*median+0.3*last_hop, low=avg*1.1, then last_hop+buffer with failed-floor+25, then config default), manual_rebalance/execute_rebalance RPC paths with atomic budget reservation, and the defibrillator diagnostic. JobManager is a stripped stub keeping only source_failure_counts.
components.rust_considerations: Two locks (_pending_lock, _cache_lock P2-005: never held across RPC — map to Mutex around O(1) map ops). Budget reservation in execute_rebalance: reserve BEFORE execute, release only when failure is NOT sweepable-pending (payment_pending AND payment_hash) — P4-009 double-spend guard. Capex candidates use candidate.max_budget_sats as limit (min'd with daily only when daily>0); hot-channel override capped at effective daily budget. Weekly-vs-daily blocker attribution: remaining>0 and remaining<limit => weekly. int truncation semantics in max_fee_sats = int(amount*(fee-est-src)/1e6), floor to 100 when <=0.

components.name: RebalanceEngineV2 orchestrator
components.responsibility: Wires state snapshot -> planner -> router pricing -> concurrent native execution -> audit/history. Owns the sats-EV gate (final_score_sats = p_success*EFV - fee - source_opportunity - failure_penalty - activity_penalty; hold-margin rejection 'below_hold_margin'; zero-cost routes always pass), per-attempt fee ceiling (pair_fee_cap_ppm, audit F1), probability-adjusted budget, in-memory pair futility (3 failures / 30 min window per (src,dst)), persisted per-kind pair cooldowns (temporary_channel_failure 300s, fee_insufficient 1800s, incorrect_cltv 3600s, permanent 21600s, payment_pending_timeout 3600s, local_execution_failed/other 600s), fill-fraction-scaled dest cooldown (base*min(1, fill_fraction*2), audit F7) with drift override (ratio drop >= 0.30 bypasses), reserve/spend/release budget rail, exclusion + partial-amount retries, pending-settlement reconciliation via listsendpays, orphan exclude-layer sweep, in-flight-dest guard (P4-008), single-flight cycle lock (run_cycle skips 'cycle_already_running'; execute_candidate fails 'engine_busy'), optional governed/arbitrated reservation (econ_* flags, fail-closed on reserve, fail-open on arbitration).
components.rust_considerations: ThreadPoolExecutor with as_completed(timeout=120): abandoned un-cancellable workers keep running and self-clean via inflight-dest counts — in Rust use tasks + timeout that must NOT cancel the reserve+pay critical section (payment already sent); replicate the counted (not set) inflight map. Budget reservation: pass FULL unified budget to reserve_budget (P4-016 — the DB BEGIN IMMEDIATE sums non-rebalance categories once; pre-subtracting external costs double-counts). Hold-on-pending (P4-007): keep reservation only when payment_pending AND payment_hash; no-hash pending is recorded 'failed' and released. Budget blocks record history status 'skipped' (not 'failed') so dest success-rate aggregates (status IN ('success','failed')) are unpolluted, and never charge futility/cooldowns. Float math in sats-EV rounds to 6 decimals — replicate rounding for parity tests. Ceil idiom -(-a*b//c) and (x+999_999)//1_000_000 must stay integer-exact.

components.name: RebalancePlanner + pair types
components.responsibility: Pure pair-based candidate selection: classify channels by per-channel target band (over-local sources vs over-remote dests), generate all cross pairs (skipping same-peer), amount = min(source excess to its band_high, dest need to its band_low, max_chunk), pair_budget = max(dest capex remaining, ceil(amount*pair_fee_cap_ppm/1e6)), additive score = 0.30*dest_urgency + 0.20*source_drain + 0.20*dest_value_class + cheap_return ((5000-min(5000,inbound_ppm))/50000). Greedy one-pair-per-channel selection up to max_pairs; every unselected channel gets a SkipRecord; publishes residual DrainDemand (only input allowed to justify Boltz loop-outs).
components.rust_considerations: Pure and deterministic — easiest module to port with golden-file parity tests. Value-class score table {profitable:2, active:1, funded:1, neutral:0}. sort by score desc must be stable for tie parity. int(round(ratio*capacity)) uses Python banker's rounding — use round-half-even in Rust.

components.name: RebalanceRouterV3 (askrene pricing)
components.responsibility: Prices a circular pair via askrene getroutes: middle query is source_peer -> dest_peer for amount+final_hop_fee, with local source/dest SCIDs (plus retry exclusions) disabled via throwaway 'rebalance-exclude-{ts}-{n}' layers, always appending 'auto.no_mpp_support'. Validates the middle path never loops through our node, translates getroutes hops (short_channel_id_dir/next_node_id) to sendpay format (channel/direction/id), reprices amounts, and wraps with our pinned first hop (source channel, fee/CLTV from source-peer policy) and final hop (dest channel, amount_sats*1000, invoice final CLTV). Returns RouteResult{route_cost_sats, route, probability_ppm}. v2 router survives only as fee/CLTV policy-lookup helpers (listpeerchannels-first, gossip fallback) shared by v3.
components.rust_considerations: getroutes needs a 45s RPC timeout (GETROUTES_RPC_TIMEOUT_SECONDS — MCF solver exceeds the global 15s ceiling on 2M-sat queries). Cycle-scoped caches are THREAD-LOCAL by design (begin_cycle/end_cycle on the cycle thread; manual RPC-thread pricings stay per-call ephemeral) — in Rust scope them to a cycle context object, not globals. Exclude-layer names must be unique across concurrent workers (itertools.count -> AtomicU64). 'Unknown layer' getroutes error must invalidate+teardown caches; orphan layer sweep at startup removes 'rebalance-exclude-*'. Error translation table (_translate_getroutes_error) maps CLN C-source messages to unknown_source_node/unknown_dest_node/unknown_layer/askrene_child_died/no_route — preserve exact substrings. Cheapest route = min by (first_hop_amt - delivered).

components.name: NativeRouteExecutor
components.responsibility: Executes the exact priced route: strict route validation (first hop == source scid, final hop == dest scid and our node id, final amount == amount_sats*1000, monotone non-increasing amounts, fee <= max_fee_sats), then invoice (expiry 300s) -> sendpay(route, payment_hash, bolt11, payment_secret) -> waitsendpay(timeout 60s). Computes actual fee = amount_sent_msat - amount_msat. On unresolved payments (waitsendpay code 200, proxy timeout, waitsendpay_status=pending) sets payment_pending=True, keeps invoice/payment, returns planned fee. On terminal failure: delpay+delinvoice cleanup, derives excluded_channels ('scid/dir' from erring_channel/direction, else all middle hops for liquidity/fee failures) and records confidence-weighted segment observations (0.85 attributed, /2 direction-unknown, 0.85/n floor 0.2 inferred). ExecutionResult + stable_failure_reason mapping live in rebalance_execution.py.
components.rust_considerations: payment_pending semantics are safety-critical: never delete invoice/payment or emit exclusions when pending; downstream never retries on top of a pending payment. Error strings are contracts: 'native_route_invalid: <reason>', 'native_route_over_budget: X > Y', 'native_sendpay_error: <msg> (failcode=..., erring_channel=...)', 'payment_pending_timeout: <msg>' — the engine's failure-kind classifier and stable_failure_reason match by prefix/substring. Invoice label format 'rebal-native-{ms}-{dest_scid}'. Malformed invoice response (NX-4) is terminal with best-effort delinvoice. fee_ppm uses integer floor division on msat.

components.name: Rebalance modes + route policy tables
components.responsibility: Data tables. MODES maps mode name -> (priority, budget_bucket, reserve_on_rail, account_costs, deadline, accounting_owner): normal/hot_protection/structural_drain/diagnostic reserve on the unified rail with engine accounting; manual (priority 100) skips reservation, caller accounts. engine_kwargs(mode) yields the reserve_budget/account_costs booleans. Route policy is a frozen stub: every pair is MARKET_ONLY/EV_POSITIVE (coordinated policies retired 2026-07) but RouteDecision's shape is kept for debug-dict stability.
components.rust_considerations: Trivial enums/const tables. Preserve the documented spec contradiction: diagnostic is a BOUNDED spend (reserved on rail), not 'no spend'.

components.name: SegmentObservationStore
components.responsibility: Thread-safe in-memory ring (max 200, TTL 900s) of failure-derived route-segment observations (scid, direction 0/1, amount bucketed to fixed powers-of-scale buckets 50k..10M, failure_class in {liquidity,fee,timeout,unknown}, confidence clamped 0..1). Engine exports a validated snapshot to CLN datastore key ['revenue','segment-observations'] after failed executions.
components.rust_considerations: Mutex<Vec> or VecDeque; observation_id format 'obs-{ts}-{seq}'. bucket = largest bucket <= amount (0 if below 50k... actually 50k is floor bucket; amounts <50k still map to 50k? No: loop starts at bucket=50k and amount<50k breaks immediately, returning 50k — amounts 1..49_999 bucket to 50_000; <=0 returns 0. Preserve exactly). Export snapshot prunes+persists sort by observed_at desc.

components.name: Defibrillator diagnostic (within rebalancer.py)
components.responsibility: Channel liveness probe before zombie closure: (1) set bounded_low_fee probe flag in DB, (2) 'active shock' 50,000-sat rebalance into the channel through the shared v2 engine with mode 'diagnostic' (reserve_budget=True, account_costs=True). Fee envelope: max(1, min(diagnostic_rebalance_max_fee_sats [default 400], daily_budget_sats, 10_000 static ceiling)); max_fee_ppm DERIVED as ceil(cap/50000*1e6) so the sat cap is the single binding knob (ruling D4). Tries up to 3 sources ranked by spendable_sats desc, advancing ONLY on route-availability failures (route_pricing_failed / native_route_invalid / no_route — no payment attempted); any other failure or pending stops the sequence. Reports honest shock_status: completed | pending | blocked (capital controls OR engine 'local_budget_block') | failed.
components.rust_considerations: Because account_costs=True, the engine records fee + updates the shared history row — the diagnostic caller must NOT record the fee again (P4-025 double-count hazard). Sources must have spendable_sats > 100_000 to qualify. expected_profit_sats hardcoded -50. Message strings ('Defibrillator active: Zero-Fee flag set + Shock {status}') surface in RPC responses.

external_dependencies: CLN RPC: listpeerchannels (balances, updates.local/remote fee policy, HTLC counts)
CLN RPC: listfunds (reserve check, channel balances)
CLN RPC: listchannels (gossip fallback for last-hop fee)
CLN RPC: getinfo (node id)

CLN askrene plugin (v24.08+): getroutes, askrene-listlayers, askrene-create-layer, askrene-update-channel, askrene-remove-layer (v24.11 throwaway exclude layers) — fail-closed: no askrene means no rebalancing
CLN RPC: invoice, sendpay, waitsendpay, listsendpays, delpay, delinvoice
CLN RPC: datastore (segment-observation snapshot export, key ['revenue','segment-observations'])

SQLite via modules/database.py: rebalance_history, rebalance_costs, budget reservations (reserve_budget BEGIN IMMEDIATE / mark_budget_spent / release_budget_reservation / cleanup_stale_reservations), failure counts, pair cooldowns (record/clear_pair_rebalance_failure, get_pair_rebalance_cooldown), post-rebalance anchors, historical inbound fee, flow windows
Python libs: pyln.client (Plugin, RpcError), concurrent.futures ThreadPoolExecutor, threading, dataclasses — no third-party deps

Injected providers: external_liquidity_cost_provider (Boltz costs), global_budget_limit_provider, capex_engine, profitability_analyzer, policy_manager, data_service (caching ThreadSafeRpcProxy with per-call timeout kwarg)
lens_maps.lens: rebalance stack: candidate selection -> askrene pricing -> sendpay execution -> budget reservation, futility/cooldowns, defibrillator
lens_maps.port_notes: FLOW: EVRebalancer.find_rebalance_candidates (timer) -> cleanup stale reservations -> capital controls -> engine.run_cycle. run_cycle: reconcile_pending_settlements -> find_candidates (snapshot from listpeerchannels + capex + profitability; planner.plan; drop in-flight dests; per-pair: persisted pair-cooldown check -> in-memory futility check -> router_v3.price_pair -> per-attempt ceiling min(prob-adjusted budget, ceil(amount*pair_fee_cap_ppm/1e6)) -> route_cost<=ceiling -> sats-EV hold-margin gate) -> policy gate (fail CLOSED without policy_manager) -> optional arbitration (fail OPEN) -> thread-pool _execute_pair(reserve_budget=True, account_costs=True) with 120s as_completed ceiling. _execute_pair: insert/adopt rebalance_history row (reservation_id = str(row id)) -> register inflight dest -> reserve_budget (FULL unified budget, P4-016: never pre-subtract external costs) -> executor.execute -> one exclusion retry (skip if pending) -> partial-amount retries (halving ladder floor min(orig-1, max(1000, min(5000, orig/2))), max 7 amounts, budget scaled ceil-proportionally, break on pending P8-001) -> record result -> finish budget (success: mark_budget_spent(actual fee); pending WITH payment_hash: HOLD reservation for reconcile sweep; else release). BUDGET SEMANTICS TO PRESERVE EXACTLY: reservation amount is the pair's max fee (effective_budget_sats when priced, else pair_budget_sats); budget blocks produce error prefix 'local_budget_block'/'zero_budget_blocks' -> history status 'skipped', never futility strikes; reconcile sweep (pending_settlement rows) computes fee = amount_sent_msat - amount_msat from the 'complete' listsendpays entry, records rebalance_cost, mark_budget_spent, clears pair failures; failed sweep rows release reservation + delpay. ERROR STRINGS ARE CONTRACTS (substring-matched): 'local_budget_block', 'engine_busy', 'dest_inflight', 'route_pricing_failed:', 'native_route_invalid:', 'native_route_over_budget:', 'native_sendpay_error:', 'payment_pending_timeout:', 'no_route', 'temporary_channel_failure', 'fee_insufficient', 'incorrect_cltv_expiry', 'permanent_failure', 'zero_budget_blocks_auto_rebalance', 'governor_block:'. FUTILITY/COOLDOWN CONSTANTS: in-memory pair breaker 3 failures/1800s; persisted per-kind cooldowns {tcf/no_route:300, fee_insufficient:1800, cltv:3600, permanent:21600, pending_timeout:3600, local/other:600} (DB backoff multiplies by count up to x6); NoRoutes classifies as temporary_channel_failure; dest futility breaker keyed on to_peer_id NOT scid (DEF-063, splice-stable), thresholds 4 (no_route/budget_exceeded) / 10 (other); fee escalation min(last_attempted*1.5, ev_max); dest cooldown = base*min(1, fill_fraction*2) where fill_fraction = amount/(amount+remaining_band_gap); drift override when anchor_ratio - current_ratio >= 0.30. SATS-EV CONSTANTS: EXPECTED_UTILIZATION=0.5, SOURCE_UTILIZATION_DISCOUNT=0.5, FAILURE_COST_RATE=0.25, UNVALIDATED_ADVERTISED_FEE_DISCOUNT=0.5; p_success = clamp(probability_ppm/1e6, 0.05, 0.99), no-probability prior 0.5 blended with empirical dest rate (>=3 attempts) as min(0.95, max(0.05, 0.5*(0.5+rate))); zero-cost routes always beat do-nothing (zero-budget equalization invariant). RPC SHAPES: getroutes returns routes[].path[] hops with short_channel_id_dir ('SCID/dir'), next_node_id, amount_msat (int or 'Nmsat' string — parse both), delay, and route-level probability_ppm (default 0 if absent); sendpay hops need {id, channel, direction, delay, amount_msat, style:'tlv'}; waitsendpay error code 200 = still pending; msat fields everywhere may be 'Nmsat' strings. History row lifecycle: exactly ONE rebalance_history row per rebalance — callers pass rebalance_id so the engine updates in place ('pending' -> success/failed/skipped/pending_settlement); success rows persist post_local_ratio anchor and the SETTLED amount (partial fills correct amount_sats from sendpay amount_msat). Manual mode bypasses reservations (fees still recorded and count against future budgets); manual amounts clamp to rebalance_max_amount regardless of force. Single-flight: cycle lock is non-blocking everywhere. base_to_sats_ceil for fees (never undercount cost), base_to_sats_floor for balances.

components.name: econ_types (checked domain types)
components.responsibility: Checked integer money/ratio/time/id types: Msat (u63), SignedMsat (i64), Sat, Ppm, Micro (0..1_000_000 fixed point), UnixTime, ChannelId/PeerId/IntentId regex-validated newtypes. Every bound violation or unit-mixing raises EconArithmeticError — authorization arithmetic fails closed, never wraps. Direction-specific msat->sat rounding: CEIL for fees/budgets/costs/revenue, FLOOR for capacity/balances, TOWARD-ZERO for signed deltas.
components.rust_considerations: Maps almost 1:1 to Rust newtypes with checked arithmetic (the docstring literally anticipates 'checked u64 in a future Rust port'). Preserve exact error semantics (fail closed to ARITHMETIC_OVERFLOW/SCHEMA_INVALID reason codes) and the three rounding rules, plus Micro.from_float_clamped's clamp-then-round-half-up (int(x*1e6 + 0.5)) as the only float ingress.

components.name: reason_codes
components.responsibility: Stable machine-readable reason-code catalog (18 codes), each tagged with owning layer (policy/arbiter/governor/executor/reconciliation/any) and kind (hold/rejection/deferral/clamp/failure/unknown). Codes are the conformance contract; wording is free to change.
components.rust_considerations: Enum + &'static str table. Code strings are wire-frozen (BUDGET_EXHAUSTED, PAUSED, INTENT_STALE, INTENT_SUPERSEDED, CONFLICT_CLOSE_REBALANCE, CONFLICT_DUPLICATE_OPEN, CONFLICT_REBALANCE_SWAP, AUTHORITY_LEVEL_BLOCKED, EXTERNAL_OUTCOME_UNKNOWN, etc.); intent envelope construction validates reason codes against the catalog.

components.name: econ_snapshot (canonical economic snapshot)
components.responsibility: Immutable EconomicSnapshot (NodeState + per-channel ChannelSnapshot with role/lifecycle enums, protections, confidence_micro) implementing economic_snapshot.v0 JSON schema. Provides canonical_json (sorted keys, no whitespace, ensure_ascii=False) used for all hashing/idempotency, and build_channel_snapshot mapping listpeerchannels + profitability duck types to the canonical model — missing evidence yields zero economics + zero confidence, never invented values.
components.rust_considerations: canonical_json must be byte-identical to Python json.dumps(sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=False) — idempotency keys (sha256 of it) and determinism pins depend on it. Channels auto-sorted by channel_id at construction (J3). Wire enums are UPPER_SNAKE strings, never ordinals.

components.name: cycle_context (determinism context)
components.responsibility: Frozen per-cycle context (cycle_id, cycle_time, seed, snapshot_id): the only clock and only randomness source policies may use. derive_seed(component) = first 8 bytes big-endian of sha256(f"{seed}:{component}") masked to u63; rng() returns a fresh seeded generator each call.
components.rust_considerations: derive_seed is a stable cross-language contract (pure sha256). rng() uses Python's random.Random — a Rust port cannot reproduce Mersenne-Twister streams cheaply; nothing pinned currently consumes rng() output (DTS sampler not yet seed-injected), so pin derive_seed only.

components.name: econ_intents (typed intent envelopes)
components.responsibility: IntentEnvelope: a PROPOSAL, never an authorization. Nine intent types (SET_FEE, SET_HTLC_MAX, REBALANCE, OPEN_CHANNEL, CLOSE_CHANNEL, SWAP_IN, SWAP_OUT, JOIN_LIQUIDITY_SWAP, MAINTAIN_ONCHAIN_RESERVE), deterministic idempotency key = sha256(canonical_json({intent_type,target,amount_msat,snapshot_id,budget_bucket})), intent_id = "int-" + key[:16], structured Explanation rendered as "kind: k=v, k=v", priority 0-100, to_wire/from_wire with strict schema_name/version check.
components.rust_considerations: Idempotency-key derivation must be byte-exact (canonical JSON of exactly those five fields, null for absent amount). is_expired uses >= (expiry at exactly expires_at counts as expired). Envelope validation errors are EconArithmeticError with specific messages; from_wire rejects schema mismatch.
