components.name: econ_arbiter (batch arbitration + live registry)
components.responsibility: Pure deterministic batch arbitrate(): sort all intents by the J3 ladder (precedence class asc, priority desc, expected_benefit desc, confidence desc, capital_committed asc, target asc, intent_id asc), then apply rules in order: 1 staleness (INTENT_STALE), 2 duplicate idempotency keys — best-sorted wins (INTENT_SUPERSEDED), 3 contradictory SET_FEE/SET_HTLC_MAX per (type,target), 4 REBALANCE into a CLOSE_CHANNEL target (CONFLICT_CLOSE_REBALANCE), and with extended_rules: 5 duplicate OPEN_CHANNEL per peer (CONFLICT_DUPLICATE_OPEN), 6 REBALANCE vs SWAP_OUT on one channel — SWAP_OUT outranks (CONFLICT_REBALANCE_SWAP). Also ActiveIntentRegistry: thread-locked live in-flight intent map keyed by idempotency_key, pruned on expiry, consulted at the governor boundary (check_and_register/release); extended rules read live from a provider that fails to legacy-rules-only on error.
components.rust_considerations: Determinism contract: same intents + same now => same result regardless of input order (byte-identical wire output, pinned by tests). Precedence table (contractual_obligation=0 .. growth=6) and DEFAULT_INTENT_PRECEDENCE map are wire behavior. Live registry note: in the registry, close-vs-rebalance and rebalance-vs-swap block whichever arrives SECOND (either direction), unlike batch arbitration where the loser is defined by intent type; also OPEN_CHANNEL duplicate is first-registered-wins live but best-sorted-wins in batch. Mutex needed; ArbitrationResult keeps rejected as (envelope, code, detail) triples.

components.name: governor_facade (authorization + authority levels)
components.responsibility: The sole authorization gate for governed spends. Decision order in authorize(): paused -> PAUSED; authority_check (fail closed on exception) -> AUTHORITY_LEVEL_BLOCKED; expired -> INTENT_STALE; registry conflict -> conflict code (also ledgered as intent_rejected); then reserve max_cost msat->sat CEILING via delegated reserve_spend -> BUDGET_EXHAUSTED on refusal. Zero-worst-case intents (reversible fee/HTLC changes) authorize without reservation and without a budget_reserved event. Authority ladder: observe=0 < fees=1 < liquidity=2 < capital=3; unknown configured values fail CLOSED to observe. Tokens carry reservation_id (caller's, possibly != idempotency_key — the 2026-07-13 phantom-reservation fix) plus arbitration_key for registry release.
components.rust_considerations: Holds no state of its own — delegates to injected closures (Database.reserve_budget/release). A facade is constructed per authorize call at each call site with per-loop authority requirements: fees->"fees", rebalance->"liquidity", planner/boltz/lnplus->"capital"-ish (verify per site). authority_allows lowercases/trims and defaults required to capital(3) when unknown. Ledger events must be keyed exactly: intent_authorized under env.idempotency_key, budget_reserved under the effective reservation_id.

components.name: econ_ledger (append-only event ledger + replay)
components.responsibility: Append-only sqlite event stream (own file econ_ledger.db, table econ_ledger_events) with 13 event types; corrections are new events, never updates — no update/delete surface exists. replay() reconstructs LedgerState: budget_reserved SETS reservation (idempotent re-announce), cost_recorded adds spend and decrements reservation floored at 0 (cost with no reservation still counts as spend and is flagged as anomaly — spend is never free), reservation_released zeroes, reconciliation_completed SETS reservation absolutely / optionally adds cost / terminal only when details.terminal, first terminal event wins.
components.rust_considerations: Opens a fresh sqlite connection per operation deliberately (live 2026-07-12 thread-affinity failure: 'SQLite objects created in a thread can only be used in that same thread') — rusqlite with a mutexed connection or per-call open both work, but keep timeout=5.0s busy handling. amounts_json/details_json stored with sort_keys=True. Amount values validated to i64/u63 range, bools rejected. Replay semantics are the budget-truth contract — port exactly, including anomaly strings like 'cost_recorded without reservation: {key}'.

components.name: econ_reconcile (ledger-to-DB reconciliation)
components.responsibility: Compares ledger replay against production spend_reservations truth; the LEDGER reconciles TO the DB. Classifies divergences: db_missing / ledger_stale_reservation / ledger_missing_reservation / amount_mismatch (all auto-resolvable via appended reconciliation_completed events) and unknown_outcome (execution_started with no terminal event older than 3600s) which is QUARANTINED with EXTERNAL_OUTCOME_UNKNOWN and never auto-resolved. In-flight keys are excluded from resolvable classification entirely. Also fee_intent_completeness: clusters fee_changes timestamps within 120s tolerance into cycles and compares counts against ledgered fee-cycle-*/fee-broadcast-* intents.
components.rust_considerations: DB terminal statuses are {'spent','released'}; 'active' rows drive resolution amounts (db_sats*1000). Timestamp clustering (adjacent-second fee_changes rows from one cycle, observed live) is a behavioral pin — naive per-timestamp comparison false-positives. Keys iterated sorted for determinism.

components.name: econ_ev (expected-value contract)
components.responsibility: The spec EV contract: expected_value = revenue - execution_cost - capital_cost - risk_premium in checked integer msat (bools rejected). Conversion helpers benefit_msat_from_sats and confidence_micro resolve None/NaN/invalid to ZERO — missing data fails conservatively, never optimistically. Envelope population gated by econ_ev_populated (default off keeps byte-pinned zeros).
components.rust_considerations: benefit_msat_from_sats accepts float sats and does int(round(v*1000)) — Python banker-vs-half-away rounding: round() is banker's rounding, so preserve that exactly if fixtures pin it. Micro clamp uses round() too.

components.name: econ_cycle (deterministic shadow cycle)
components.responsibility: The deterministic core of the spec cycle (collect -> snapshot -> generate intents -> arbitrate -> record -> publish) running in shadow with NO execution authority. Maps pure-planner rebalance PairCandidates (sorted by (dest,source)) to REBALANCE envelopes (600s expiry, priority 50, bucket 'rebalance', origin econ_cycle_shadow), batch-arbitrates them, and emits an econ_cycle_result v0 wire dict; run_shadow_cycle is the fail-open collector/publisher that ledgers each intent as intent_proposed with shadow_cycle:true.
components.rust_considerations: Acceptance: identical inputs + identical CycleContext produce byte-identical wire output regardless of input ordering (pinned by tests/test_econ_cycle.py). Score in explanation is round(float,6). Seed derives via base_ctx.derive_seed('econ-cycle') from seed 0 and the cycle identity.

components.name: econ_shadow (shadow assembler / governed-path hub)
components.responsibility: The wiring hub between the legacy plugin and the governed layer. Records live decisions as typed intents (record_fee_intents), journals the legacy spend lifecycle (note_spend_reserved/settled/released — settle emits cost_recorded + execution_succeeded + reservation_released because DB settle is terminal for the whole reservation), runs the parallel shadow-governor verdict on real rebalance reservations with divergence warnings, owns the shared ActiveIntentRegistry (gated by econ_arbiter_enabled, extended rules by econ_conflict_rules_extended read live), serves TTL-cached (300s) canonical snapshot refs ledgered as snapshot_created, and self-throttles the hourly reconciliation sweep. FAIL-OPEN CONTRACT: no method may raise into a caller; disabled entirely unless econ_shadow_enabled.
components.rust_considerations: Asymmetric failure semantics are the whole point: shadow/observe paths fail OPEN (swallow everything, log once at warn then debug), while governed authorization paths fail CLOSED. Flag parsing accepts string forms ('true','1','yes','on'). Ledger lives beside db_path as econ_ledger.db. Legacy spend journal reuses reservation_id as both intent_id and idempotency_key, timestamps are audit stamps (wall clock), not decision inputs.

components.name: risk_profiles (operator risk bundles + field classification)
components.responsibility: risk_profile in {preserve, conservative, balanced, growth, custom}: bundles of economic-risk config values applied at startup only to keys the operator has NOT explicitly overridden (precedence: explicit DB override > profile bundle > dataclass default). Every Config field is classified into exactly one of 7 categories (safety_invariant, authority_control, economic_risk, operational_timing, external_integration, advanced_expert, deprecated_transition); bundles may only touch economic_risk keys — safety and authority are never profile-bundled. preview_profile/preview_all are read-only diffs with a daily>weekly budget contradiction pre-check.
components.rust_considerations: Mostly static tables. Unknown/invalid profile names resolve to {} (behave like custom — fail conservative). A coverage test pins that every config field is classified; replicate that pin in Rust. Preview response shapes (would_change/blocked_by_explicit_override/already_equal/contradiction_precheck/activation) are RPC-visible.

components.name: governed execution call sites (per-loop cutover)
components.responsibility: Each execution loop has its own governed gate flag (econ_governor_rebalance/fees/planner/boltz/lnplus_enabled) and constructs a GovernorFacade per authorization with injected closures over Database.reserve_budget/release_budget_reservation, the shared registry, ledger, and an authority_check for its required level (e.g. rebalance requires 'liquidity'). Reference implementation: rebalance_engine_v2._governed_reserve_execution_budget builds the REBALANCE envelope (real EV when econ_ev_populated), ledgers intent_proposed with governed:true, calls facade.authorize(env, now, reservation_id=caller's id), and maps refusals to legacy-compatible error strings. Any internal error fails CLOSED — no spend without authorization.
components.rust_considerations: LOC estimate covers only the governed sections of these large files (fee_controller is 8.6k LOC total, rebalance_engine_v2 3.6k). Error strings are load-bearing legacy compatibility: BUDGET_EXHAUSTED maps to 'local_budget_block: {remaining} sats remaining of {budget_limit} unified budget'; other refusals to 'governor_block: {reason_code}'. Reservation happens under the CALLER's reservation_id so legacy finish paths (settle/release) work unchanged; the registry slot is the envelope idempotency_key. REBALANCE envelopes use snapshot_ref when available else synthetic 'rebalance-cycle-{now}', 600s TTL, priority 50.

lens_maps.lens: Governed economics layer: intent -> arbitration -> reservation -> execution pipeline, shadow mode, conflict rules, authority levels (Rust-port mapping)
external_dependencies: sqlite3 (stdlib) — econ_ledger.db (own file beside revenue_ops.db) and the production revenue_ops.db via injected Database methods

Database (modules/database.py): reserve_budget, release_budget_reservation, reserve_spend/settle/release lifecycle, get_spend_reservation_states, get_recent_fee_changes — the DB remains the authorization/budget authority until cutover completes
pyln-client plugin API: plugin.log only within this layer (safe_plugin wrapper); RPC handlers revenue-econ-snapshot / econ ledger status / shadow-cycle diagnostics in cl-revenue-ops.py
CLN RPCs indirectly via collectors (listpeerchannels-shaped channel dicts feed build_channel_snapshot); the governed layer itself performs no RPC
hashlib.sha256 + canonical JSON (json.dumps sort_keys, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=False) for idempotency keys and seed derivation

Config runtime flags: econ_shadow_enabled, econ_arbiter_enabled, econ_conflict_rules_extended, econ_ev_populated, econ_governor_{fees,rebalance,planner,boltz,lnplus}_enabled, econ_cycle_*_enabled, authority_level, risk_profile, paused
JSON Schemas (normative contracts): schemas/intent.v0/v1, economic_snapshot.v0/v1, ledger_event.v0, ledger_projection.v0, conformance_case.v0

Key contract docs: docs/refactor/phase0/wire-contract-spec.md (J1-J4 normative rules), docs/planning/refactor.md (architecture + core invariant), docs/planning/refactor-gap-closure.md, docs/refactor/phase0/snapshot-mapping.md, ev-coverage-matrix.md, portability-hazards.md, docs/planning/2026-07-12-refactor-phase1-wiring.md and the phase2 planning docs
lens_maps.port_notes: Core invariant (docs/planning/refactor.md): no policy module may spend, change a channel/policy, or authorize its own budget — everything flows snapshot -> typed intent -> arbiter -> governor -> adapter -> ledger. A Rust port must preserve, byte-for-byte where pinned: (1) canonical JSON = Python json.dumps(sort_keys=True, separators=(\",\",\":\"), ensure_ascii=False); idempotency_key = sha256 hex of exactly {intent_type, target, amount_msat(null ok), snapshot_id, budget_bucket}; intent_id = \"int-\"+key[:16]; token_id = \"auth-\"+key[:16]. (2) The J3 tie-break ladder and precedence classes (contractual_obligation 0 .. growth 6); arbitrate() is order-insensitive and byte-deterministic (tests/test_econ_cycle.py pins wire output). (3) Rounding: msat->sat CEIL for fees/budgets/costs/revenue, FLOOR for capacity/balances, toward-zero for signed deltas; governor reserves max_cost with CEILING so budgets never undercount; zero-worst-case reversible intents authorize WITHOUT a reservation or budget_reserved event. (4) Governor decision order and reason codes: PAUSED, AUTHORITY_LEVEL_BLOCKED (authority ladder observe<fees<liquidity<capital, unknown level fails closed to observe, required defaults to capital), INTENT_STALE (now >= expires_at), registry conflict codes, BUDGET_EXHAUSTED. Governed paths reserve under the CALLER's reservation_id (2026-07-13 phantom-reservation fix) while ledgering intent_authorized under the envelope idempotency_key. (5) Ledger replay semantics including the spend-never-free anomaly rule, first-terminal-wins, reconciliation_completed SETS reservation absolutely; append-only — corrections are new events. (6) Reconciliation: ledger reconciles TO the DB; unknown outcomes (execution_started, no terminal, >3600s) quarantine with EXTERNAL_OUTCOME_UNKNOWN and are never auto-resolved; fee-completeness clusters timestamps within 120s. (7) Failure asymmetry: shadow/observe paths fail OPEN (never raise into callers), authorization paths fail CLOSED (exception => no spend); extended-conflict-rule provider errors fall back to legacy rules, never stricter. (8) Legacy-compatible error strings on refusal: 'local_budget_block: {remaining} sats remaining of {budget_limit} unified budget' and 'governor_block: {reason_code}'. (9) Live registry conflict semantics differ from batch (second-arriving intent blocked regardless of type for close/rebalance and rebalance/swap; first-registered OPEN_CHANNEL wins) — port both behaviors, don't unify. (10) Feature flags default OFF; 'custom' risk profile derives nothing (behavior-preserving migration default); profile bundles touch only economic_risk keys. sqlite gotcha: ledger opens a connection per operation because of thread-affinity failures observed live; the layer is called from multiple plugin threads, so ActiveIntentRegistry needs a real mutex. Float hazards are already quarantined: floats only enter via clamped/conservative converters (Micro.from_float_clamped, benefit_msat_from_sats — note Python round() is banker's rounding) — everything authoritative is checked integer math.

lens_maps.lens: Capital allocation subsystem: channel open/close planning, ROI gates, zombie/defibrillator flow, Boltz swap cycles, LN+ swap automation, and unified budget interactions

components.name: CapexBudgetEngine
components.responsibility: Pure-calculation unified capex budget engine: computes per-channel 30d budgets (tiers proven/active/bootstrap/fleet/blocked with tier_ppm caps 2000/500/250), fleet exploration budget (channel opens), tactical budget (Boltz treasury), and fleet priority class (defensive/preservation/operational/growth). Also owns the Boltz reservation lifecycle: reserve_boltz_swap_budget -> settle_boltz_swap_reservation -> release, and record_boltz_spend into the unified 'boltz' spend category (subcategory swap_fee vs structural).
components.rust_considerations: All internal arithmetic in msat (i64), sats only at boundaries with CEILING rounding (base_to_sats_ceil; zero msat -> zero sats, no false floor). CB-4 fail-closed: DB read error -> None (never empty dict) -> db_degraded=True zeroes ALL budgets that cycle; must log 'capex_budget: DB degraded this cycle — failing closed'. Budget formula invariants: proven = max(0, contribution_30d*rate - capex_30d) (30d window on BOTH sides, audit F1/F1b); proven gate is contribution_30d > 100 sats (F1c); roi_mult = clamp(1+marginal_roi, 0.25, 1.5), neutral 1.0 when marginal_roi_reliable is False (F6/F8); dead-capital efficiency multiplier 0.0, floored at 0.25 for gateway-value channels (F5: role==inbound_gateway or lifetime sourced fees > 100_000 msat); fleet defensive flip requires >1 hard bleeder OR >10% of capacity (F4b). Envelope: min(capex_global_envelope, daily*30, weekly*30/7), proportional scale-down of all budgets when over. attribute_boltz_cost 50/50 split (integer floor/remainder) is INFORMATIONAL only — record_boltz_spend writes the full fee once, keyed boltz:{swap_id} INSERT OR REPLACE (idempotent journal re-settles). settle_boltz_swap_reservation (P4-019): release the reservation ONLY after the spend event persisted; on write failure keep reservation active and return False.

components.name: CapacityPlanner
components.responsibility: Timer-driven capital allocation engine. execute_cycle: fee gate -> identify winners (effective ROI>20 after rebal penalty, turnover>0.5, |flow_ratio|>0.8) and losers (ZOMBIE/FIRE SALE/STAGNANT/DEAD_CAPITAL) -> redeployment-EV demotion -> bounded defibrillations (diagnostic rebalances) -> bounded closes -> portfolio-balance governor -> 5-strategy candidate discovery with fixed-anchor score normalization and pool quotas -> EV-gated channel opens (LN+ evaluator competes for the same open slot) -> recycle-opportunity nomination that feeds Boltz coordination (preferred loop-out SCID, funding deficit, loser SCIDs).
components.rust_considerations: Largest and most invariant-dense module. Zombie/defibrillator flow: a CLOSE requires >=2 diagnostic rebalance attempts in 14d, UNLESS is_hard_bleeder (bypass) or defib is policy-impossible (rebalance_mode disabled/source_only waives the prerequisite — deadlock guard); kalman regime change demotes CLOSE->DEFIBRILLATE except for hard bleeders; remote-opened channels only close below -75% marginal ROI; flow-data absence demotes to DEFIBRILLATE. Dead-capital stage machine: none -> fee_reduction (delegated to fee controller; stage timer starts ONLY if the delegation planner-action record succeeds) -> defibrillation -> close, per-stage timeout 24h local / 48h remote, close blocked by protection or by zero executed defibs (F4b), persisted stage demotable. ROI gates: open EV = 180d forecast revenue (anchored to node median observed daily ppm * 0.5 new-peer discount, ceiling 45 ppm/day) - open cost (feerate*140vB) - close cost (mutual_close feerate*200vB, fallback unilateral then opening) - rebalance cost (volume model shared via ASSUMED_AVG_FEE_PPM=150) - capital hurdle (planner_min_annual_roi_pct). Recycle EV: candidate_ev - marginal_30d*6 - close_cost, min 5000 sats; redeployment EV mirrors it exactly (P5-002: NO max/negation — negative residual favors close). Score anchors/floors (SCALE_ANCHORS, RAW_SCORE_FLOORS winner>=0.20, STRATEGY_WEIGHTS) and pool quotas (graph 4, route_pair 4, pool cap 32, prune < -3.0) are behavioral constants. Failed-open exponential backoff 2^N hours capped 168h, streak reset by completed/dry_run; peer exposure cap 2x planner_max_channel_sats. Portfolio governor bands 70/80/95% local; constrained/blocked allow only sink-adjacent or option_will_fund opens; discovery still runs when blocked. Budget rail: reserve_spend BEFORE fundchannel/close with live effective_budget_sats + since_timestamp (window_hours clamp 1-168); settle via mark_spend_reservation_spent with 3 loud retries, on persistent failure LEAVE reservation active (never release); release on failure. Policy gates fail CLOSED (missing policy_manager blocks open/close/defib); reason strings are pinned by tests/golden/fixtures/close_protection/. 'below min chan size of X BTC' error parsed by regex for one bounded retry at peer minimum. LN+ reserved sats debit available_sats before sizing and inside _check_reserve. SCID normalization ':'->'x' everywhere.

components.name: BoltzCliManager
components.responsibility: Synchronous boltzcli subprocess wrapper for boltzd: quotes, loop_in (submarine), loop_out (reverse, with CLN first-hop pinning via external-pay + pay excludes when chanIds unsupported), chainswap, refund/claim/withdraw, wallet ops. Enforces layered budget gates (daily quote budget, tactical, per-channel capex, atomic unified pre-create reservation) and maintains a JSON swap journal for spend accounting and idempotent fee recording.
components.rust_considerations: Subprocess orchestration with three locks: _journal_lock, _ignored_swaps_lock, _swap_creation_lock (P0-1: budget-check + create serialized to kill TOCTOU). Exact error-string matching required: 'chanIds are not supported for cln' (case-insensitive contains) detected in exceptions, JSON results, AND async swapinfo probes -> caches _reverse_chanids_supported=False, retries without pinning after re-checking budget against the NESTED quote. Fee estimation: named fields (boltzFee/networkFee/serviceFee/routingFee/onchainFee) authoritative when present; else recursive sum of *fee* fields where a level with numeric fee fields STOPS descent (E-4.4 anti-double-count). Reverse routing fee estimate = ceil(amount*limit_ppm/1e6), 0 when limit unset. State classification: 'abandoned' contains 'done' but is NOT completed; completed tokens success/completed/claimed/done; terminal adds refund/abandon/error/failed. get_boltz_cost_components must NEVER call global_budget_limit_provider (mutual recursion -> boltzcli fork bomb); reserved estimate capped at remaining budget. Pre-create unified reservation returns three-valued: id (reserved), None (enforcement N/A, fail-open on infra error), False (budget exceeded -> reject with error 'unified_budget_exceeded'); loop_out stashes context in _pending_swap_budget so the wrapper settles/releases on every exit including exceptions. Structural loop-outs bypass the per-channel capex gate ONLY when structural_envelope_provider() > 0 (fail closed). Self-contained bech32/bech32m/base58check/blech32 address validation (BIP173/350 polymod, double-SHA256 checksum) before any subprocess; '--' argv terminator on all free-form positionals; withdraw hard cap max_withdraw_sats (default 10M), sweep requires confirm_sweep=true. Journal: atomic tmp+rename writes, prune 180d/200 entries; invoice payment guard: never pay an invoice with no decodable amount or amount > requested swap sats.

components.name: BoltzAutoCycle
components.responsibility: In-plugin orchestrator (roughly lines 1040-2270 plus the plan builders/executors it calls) that runs one Boltz cycle: fetch planner + rebalancer coordination signals, build an expansion-treasury plan (refill on-chain reserve toward target by draining >=80% local channels) and/or a balance plan (profitability-gated loop-in/loop-out candidates), select mode treasury/balance/idle, execute up to max_actions swaps, with a fall-through from a zero-executed treasury cycle to the balance cycle so the structural envelope gets a turn.
components.rust_considerations: Single-flight non-blocking lock; state dict (running, last_trigger, consecutive_errors, last_error) — consecutive_errors resets ONLY on status executed/dry_run (C3), increments on error results and exceptions. dry_run defaults False for BOTH daemon and manual RPC (DD4 operator ruling 2026-07-02). Mode selection: treasury wins only when status=='ok' AND at least one EXECUTABLE rec (passes_profit_guard and NOT structural — counting structural recs deadlocks the cycle); else balance if it has recommendations; else idle with reason 'no_eligible_boltz_actions' (surfaces 'no_profitability_data (N channels)' when applicable). Currency selection quotes BTC vs LBTC and prefers LBTC on ties/failures. Coordination contract from planner: {loser_scids (avoid loop-out through doomed channels), funding_deficit_sats, best_candidate, preferred_loop_out_scid/reason, cycle_ts}; rebalancer adds rebalancer_exhausted/depleted_count. One pending-swap listswaps count shared across all plan builds per cycle; selection-time plans reused by executors (never rebuilt — quote subprocess cost).

components.name: LNPlusSwapAutomation
components.responsibility: LN+ (lightningnetwork.plus) liquidity-swap automation in three collaborators: LNPlusClient (HTTPS + signmessage auth, gate 15 challenge validation), SwapEvaluator (gates 0-9: fill state, terms, peer quality with fail-closed ban lookups, ring-assignment inference, EV vs best regular open with preference margin, funds/capex gates, at most one application per cycle), and SwapLifecycle (gates 10-14: circuit breaker, reconcile/backfill, 48h-deadline channel opens with atomic budget reservation, no_close protection activation for BOTH ring sides, mid-contract vanish watch, finalize with ratings and defection tracking).
components.rust_considerations: Applying to a swap is an IRREVERSIBLE COMMITMENT (48h fundchannel deadline once filled); every DB transition is intent-before-external-call, outcome-after. Circuit breaker in config_overrides key '_lnplus_breaker': trip preserves the FIRST reason (B10), missed deadline / divergence trips it; breaker blocks new applications but NEVER obligation phases. Reconcile semantics are exact: 'applied' rows get a 600s grace window (B9); applied-but-vanished => cancelled_remote (terminal, frees slot — NOT a breaker trip, B4); applied-but-LN+-completed => breaker; opening/opened divergence => breaker; LN+ pending/opening ghosts with no local row => breaker, except pending ghosts matching local terminal statuses (failed/withdrawn/cancelled_remote) which get delete_application cleanup (B5b). Backfill runs once (double-checked lock + flag) and is per-swap idempotent (skip existing rows — lnplus_record_swap is INSERT OR REPLACE). Open idempotency matches existing channel by capacity via total_msat OR to_us_msat (B7 dual-fund) — never by peer alone (I5b); terminal local rows never resurrected by stale LN+ 'opening' listings (C2). Feerate by deadline slack: slow >24h, normal >12h, urgent otherwise. All API strings validated before RPC use: pubkey regex ^0[23][0-9a-fA-F]{64}$, connect addr charset/length, challenge must be printable, <=500 chars, not lnbc/lntb/lnbcrt/cbor/psbt-prefixed. TZ-less ISO timestamps are UTC (B3). no_close tag ownership: tag_added/incoming_tag_added=1 only when WE added it; release only when flag==1 AND no other active contract references the peer in either role (C-3); policy lookup failure => protect but stamp 0 (F3b). Finalize: listpeerchannels failure returns None => defer, no state change (B2 — never file negative rating on RPC hiccup); rating retry window 7d past ends_at via ended_rating_pending. EV: reuses planner _calculate_open_ev for both outbound and inbound corridor (I4: no extra open-cost subtraction), inbound_credit = min(corridor, capacity*0.005), reliability floor 0.6 + ratings, Tor-only discount 0.8, lockup haircut 0.3*best_regular_ev*(months/12); swap preferred unless regular EV beats it beyond lnplus_swap_preference_margin. Constants NEG_RATIO_MAX=0.10, SCORE_FLOOR=0.5, cancelled/banned participants excluded everywhere (B12).

external_dependencies: CLN RPCs: listpeerchannels, listchannels (source/destination), listnodes, listfunds, feerates (perkb/perkw), getinfo, fundchannel (incl. dual-fund request_amt/compact_lease), close (with feerange), pay (with channel-scoped exclude scid/dir list, retry_for), listpays, decode/decodepay, connect, signmessage

boltzcli subprocess (against local boltzd): quote, createswap, createreverseswap (--chan-id/--external-pay/--routing-fee-limit-ppm/--to-wallet), createchainswap, swapinfo, listswaps (--json/--manual/--pending), refundswap, claimswaps, wallet list/send/receive, swapmnemonic get; optional sudo -n -u <user> prefix

LN+ HTTPS API v2 (https://lightningnetwork.plus/api/2): get_message, get_applicable_swaps, get_swap, get_my_swaps, create_application, delete_application, complete_application, get_notifications, mark_read_notifications, create_rating — form-encoded POST with signmessage zbase auth

SQLite via modules/database.py: reserve_spend (BEGIN IMMEDIATE atomic cross-category budget), mark_spend_reservation_spent, release_spend_reservation, record_spend_event, get_spend_ledger_summary, get_total_capex_by_channel, planner_actions/planner_candidates tables, lnplus_* tables, config_overrides, dead_capital_stages, peer reputation/uptime/inbound-fee history, route pairs
JSON files under boltzd datadir: cl_revenue_ops_swap_journal.json, cl_revenue_ops_ignored_external_swaps.json (atomic tmp+os.replace writes)
Python stdlib only otherwise: subprocess, threading, urllib, hashlib, json, statistics.median; pyln.client Plugin for logging/RPC
lens_maps.port_notes: Invariants a Rust port must preserve exactly. (1) Budget semantics: one unified cross-category 24h budget rail; every money-committing action (open, close, boltz swap, LN+ open) does reserve_spend with live effective_budget_sats BEFORE the irreversible call, settles the actual cost as a spend event afterward, releases only on failure; settle failures are loud with 3 bounded retries and the reservation is LEFT ACTIVE on persistent failure (never released — overspend-permitting direction). Boltz spend events are keyed 'boltz:{swap_id}' and INSERT OR REPLACE (idempotent: estimate later replaced by actual). Capex engine is fail-closed: any DB read failure zeroes all budgets for the cycle (db_degraded). All budget math in msat with ceiling conversion to sats at boundaries. (2) Error-string contracts: 'chanIds are not supported for cln' (substring, case-insensitive) drives the reverse-swap capability cache and the without-pinning retry across three detection paths (exception, JSON result, async swapinfo probe); 'min chan size of ([0-9.]+) BTC' regex drives the one-shot open retry at peer minimum; 'unified_budget_exceeded' is a returned error value; skip/rejection reason strings (e.g. 'rebalance_mode=... forbids filling — defib blocked', gate:reason strings like 'terms:existing channel to assigned outbound peer', close-protection reasons) are pinned by golden-fixture tests and consumed by other gates via substring checks ('rebalance_mode=' waives the defib prerequisite). (3) RPC response shapes: msat fields may be int, 'Nmsat' string, or nested — parse_msat semantics everywhere; boltzcli swap payloads normalized through wrapper keys (swap/reverseSwap/chainSwap/channelCreation) with nested-precedence merge and id-dedup; LN+ responses may wrap single objects in one-element arrays (unwrap at the client boundary, string-normalize all swap ids). (4) State machines: dead-capital fee_reduction->defibrillation->close with 24/48h stage timeouts and executed-defib + protection preconditions; LN+ applied->opening->opened->active->ended(+ended_rating_pending) with breaker/reconcile rules and terminal statuses never resurrected; boltz swap terminality ('abandoned' is terminal-not-completed). (5) Concurrency: swap creation, journal writes, watcher pass, auto-cycle run, and reconcile-backfill are each serialized by dedicated locks; SQLite WAL single-writer BEGIN IMMEDIATE is the atomicity anchor the whole DD1 design leans on — a Rust port must keep budget check+reserve inside one transaction. (6) Fail direction is deliberate per gate: policy gates, ban lookups, structural envelope, capex lookups, and governed reservations fail CLOSED; existing-channel checks, arbitration, recycle-EV errors, and unified-reserve infra errors fail OPEN (documented per call site). Float math appears only in scoring/EV heuristics (safe to reproduce with f64); all money is integer sats/msat.

lens_maps.lens: database-layer (modules/database.py, 7962 LOC): SQLite schema, migrations, threading/WAL model, reservation/ledger tables, and msat-vs-sat conventions for the Rust port

components.name: connection-and-threading
components.responsibility: Thread-local SQLite connection factory plus shutdown cleanup. Each thread gets its own connection via threading.local(); connections are opened with isolation_level=None (autocommit, explicit BEGIN IMMEDIATE for transactions) and check_same_thread=False solely so close_all_connections() can close other threads' connections at shutdown. Per-connection PRAGMAs: journal_mode=WAL (with fallback warning if not 'wal'), busy_timeout=5000, synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON, auto_vacuum=INCREMENTAL, then best-effort cache_size=-16000, mmap_size=128MB, temp_store=MEMORY.
components.rust_considerations: rusqlite with a per-thread connection pool or a single-writer actor. Preserve: BEGIN IMMEDIATE semantics as the cross-writer serialization primitive; busy_timeout=5000ms; wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) on shutdown (both close_main_connection and close_all_connections); PRAGMA failure on the perf pragmas must be non-fatal while failure on the core pragmas closes the connection and raises. A tracked-connections list guarded by a mutex mirrors _thread_connections/_thread_conn_lock (EH-6).

components.name: schema-init-and-migrations
components.responsibility: Creates ~35 tables and ~35 indexes idempotently and runs all migrations on every startup. Migration mechanism is deliberately versionless: schema_version is a singleton row (id=1, version=1) that is WRITE-ONLY by design (DD9/MIG-3 operator ruling 2026-07-02) — no gating, because every migration is additive/idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN wrapped in try/except sqlite3.OperationalError, or PRAGMA table_info column-presence checks). Also drops orphan tables (flow_history, portfolio_metrics, clboss_unmanaged, pid_state, hive_member_confirmations, corridor_flow_daily) and redundant indexes (idx_forwards_channels, idx_forwards_out_time, idx_planner_actions_peer).
components.rust_considerations: The port must keep the exact 'additive, idempotent, no version gate' contract so old/new binaries can share a DB. Data migrations to reproduce byte-for-byte: (1) _migrate_forwards_schema — adds resolved_time/resolution_time, dedups forwards inside one transaction, creates idx_forwards_in_time; (2) _migrate_ignored_peers_to_policies — copies ignored_peers rows into peer_policies (strategy='static', rebalance_mode='ignore', tags JSON containing 'migrated_ignore' + original reason), renames table to _backup_ignored_peers as the done-marker, re-runs if rows remain; (3) _migrate_flow_v2/_migrate_kalman/_migrate_temporal_profile — column adds via PRAGMA table_info; kalman also creates kalman_state and later columns velocity_unit ('per_day' default) and observation_count. Migration failures log 'DB migration warning: ...' at warn/debug and never abort startup.

components.name: input-validation-helpers
components.responsibility: Security validation: channel_id must match \A\d+x\d+x\d+\Z, peer_id \A[0-9a-fA-F]{66}\Z (anchored with \A/\Z specifically to reject trailing newlines). _sanitize_fee clamps to [0, 50_000] sats; _sanitize_amount allows negatives but clamps magnitude to 10_000_000_000 sats (100 BTC); both treat non-numeric/NaN/inf as 0 and log 'Security: ...' warnings.
components.rust_considerations: Clamping (not erroring) is the contract — callers rely on writes succeeding with clamped values. MAX_FEE_SATS=50000, MAX_AMOUNT_SATS=10_000_000_000. Preserve log wording ('Security: Negative {field} ... clamping to 0', 'Security: Excessive {field} ... clamping to ...') if log-scraping tests exist.

components.name: channel-state-and-kalman-store
components.responsibility: CRUD for channel_states (PK channel_id; peer_id, state 'source'/'sink'/'balanced', flow_ratio REAL, sats_in/sats_out in SATS, capacity, plus migrated REAL columns confidence/velocity/flow_multiplier/ema_decay, kalman_flow_ratio/velocity/uncertainty, forward_count, temporal_profile_json), kalman_state (PK channel_id; REAL filter state: flow_ratio, flow_velocity, variance_ratio, variance_velocity, covariance, innovation_variance, last_innovation, velocity_unit TEXT default 'per_day', observation_count), channel_probes (probe_type legacy 'zero_fee' vs current 'bounded_low_fee', 24h default max age), and plugin_flags (lazy-created key/value used for kalman purge-once flag).
components.rust_considerations: Float-heavy (Kalman covariances, flow ratios stored as SQLite REAL/f64) — keep f64 end to end; do not round-trip through f32. Batch upsert methods (update_channel_states_batch, save_kalman_states_batch, save_temporal_profiles_batch) commit in one transaction. kalman_purge_needed/mark_kalman_purge_done implement a run-once flag via plugin_flags CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS at call time.

components.name: fee-strategy-and-audit
components.responsibility: fee_strategy_state (PK channel_id): last_revenue_rate REAL (rate-based, replaced legacy last_revenue_sats INTEGER — get_fee_strategy_state handles old-schema rows), last_fee_ppm, trend_direction (1/-1), step_ppm (default 50), consecutive_same_direction, last_broadcast_fee_ppm, is_sleeping/sleep_until/stable_cycles (hysteresis), last_state (default 'balanced'), forward_count_since_update, last_volume_sats, v2_state_json (JSON blob holding DTS+PID posterior/integral state — this replaced the dropped pid_state table). fee_changes append-only audit log: old/new_fee_ppm, reason, manual flag, reason_code, heuristic_modifiers (explainability), 90-day retention.
components.rust_considerations: v2_state_json is an opaque JSON contract shared with the fee controller — the Rust port must parse/emit the same JSON shape (serde_json with unknown-field preservation is safest). Legacy-row handling in get_fee_strategy_state (last_revenue_sats int → last_revenue_rate float) must survive for old DBs. Defaults when no row exists (step_ppm 50, trend 1) are behavior, not just schema defaults.

components.name: rebalance-history-and-failure-tracking
components.responsibility: rebalance_history: amount_sats, max_fee_sats, actual_fee_sats (legacy sat), actual_fee_msat (added by the sats->msat migration), expected/actual_profit_sats, status ('pending', 'success', 'failed', plus 'pending_settlement' for unresolved payments parked with payment_hash for the reconciliation sweep), rebalance_type ('normal'/'diagnostic'), reason_code, bleeder_status, post_local_ratio REAL. channel_failures and pair_rebalance_failures (PK source+dest, failure_kind, cooldown_until) persist backoff/cooldown across restarts. Partial indexes: idx_rh_pending_settlement (WHERE status='pending_settlement') and idx_rh_success_to_channel (WHERE status='success').
components.rust_considerations: Readers use COALESCE(actual_fee_msat, COALESCE(actual_fee_sats,0)*1000) everywhere — the port must keep both columns and the coalesce order (msat wins) or historical rows lose precision/vanish. get_rebalance_history_by_peer returns fee_paid_msat and amount_msat (amount_sats*1000) in its response shape. cleanup_orphaned_rebalances flips stale 'pending' rows. The status string set is a cross-module contract.

components.name: budget-reservations-and-spend-ledger
components.responsibility: The money-safety core. Three tables, all SAT-denominated: budget_reservations (rebalance budget: reservation_id PK, reserved_sats, reserved_at, job_channel_id, status 'active'/'spent'/'released'), spend_reservations (generic ledger with category/subcategory/reference_id/channel_id/metadata_json, same status set), spend_events (committed spend: event_id PK, category, amount_sats, source, never pruned — accounting source of truth). Both reserve paths run entirely inside BEGIN IMMEDIATE so the single WAL writer lock serializes them against each other (DD1/P1-003: no cross-category TOCTOU).
components.rust_considerations: Exact semantics to preserve: unified committed total = SUM(active spend_reservations, NO time filter) + SUM(active budget_reservations, NO time filter — P4-017: aged-but-active holds count in full) + SUM(rebalance_costs.cost_sats since window) + SUM(spend_events.amount_sats since window); daily check first, optional weekly check second; on rejection ROLLBACK and return (False, remaining) with remaining possibly negative; on success remaining = min(daily, weekly) - amount. reserve_spend extras: terminal-state guard — a reservation_id already 'spent'/'released' is REFUSED resurrection (COMMIT + warn log 'Spend reservation {rid} already {status}; refusing to resurrect to active', return False); re-reserving an 'active' id REPLACES it and only the delta counts (existing_active_sats subtracted); category lowercased/trimmed; metadata JSON serialized with sort_keys=True; rejection log format 'Spend reservation {rid} rejected: unified budget {N} would be exceeded (requested {a} + committed/reserved {b})'. Optional spend_journal hooks fire only AFTER successful state changes and are exception-guarded — they can never affect an authorization outcome. cleanup releases stale 'active' reservations after 14400s (budget) / 86400s (spend). get_budget_status/get_daily_rebalance_spend/get_spend_ledger_summary response dict shapes (total_spent_sats, total_reserved_sats, spent_24h_sats, ...) are RPC-visible.

components.name: forwards-ingestion-and-pruning
components.responsibility: The hottest write path. forwards table is msat-native (in_msat, out_msat, fee_msat, resolution_time REAL seconds, timestamp, resolved_time). bulk_insert_forwards and cleanup_old_data chunk work into BULK_WRITE_BATCH_SIZE=2000-row transactions (P2-002) so no BEGIN IMMEDIATE outlives busy_timeout. cleanup_old_data keeps ~8 days of raw forwards; before deleting, each chunk aggregates exactly the rows it deletes into daily_forwarding_stats (keyed channel_id + UTC-midnight date, out-channel view) and daily_forwarding_stats_inbound (in-channel view) in the SAME transaction via additive ON CONFLICT ... += upserts. Other retention: 90d fee_changes/rebalance_history/terminal reservations/pair cooldowns, 365d financial_snapshots; rebalance_costs, spend_events, planner_actions never pruned. Uses PRAGMA incremental_vacuum(100), never full VACUUM.
components.rust_considerations: Crash-consistency invariant: a forward row is never counted into daily stats without also being deleted in the same transaction (and vice versa) — port must keep aggregate+delete atomic per chunk. Day bucketing is (timestamp // 86400) * 86400, matching SQLite floor division. On any chunk failure: ROLLBACK, log at error, return early (next cycle retries). Every revenue/volume analytics query UNIONs live forwards with the daily rollup tables using the retention cutoff as the boundary — get this boundary wrong and revenue double-counts or drops.

components.name: revenue-pnl-analytics
components.responsibility: Read-side analytics: realized fee ppm, total routing revenue, per-channel and all-channels P&L (direct exit-channel revenue + inbound 'sourced' contribution, combined as max(revenue, sourced_fee)), size-bucket revenue histograms (micro/small/medium/large/whale at 10k/100k/500k/5M sat boundaries on out_msat/1000), hourly flow histograms, daily flow buckets, weighted volume, top route pairs, peer latency stats. All internal math is msat-native; conversion to sats happens only at the reporting boundary.
components.rust_considerations: Rounding conventions are load-bearing: base_to_sats_ceil for revenue/costs, base_to_sats_floor for volume, base_delta_to_sats_toward_zero for net P&L deltas — the Rust port must reproduce these exactly or dashboards drift from historical values. ppm math is integer: (fee_msat * 1_000_000) // out_msat. SCID alias handling (_scid_aliases): every read matches canonical 'AxBxC', the raw input, and legacy colon form 'A:B:C' via IN (...) lists. get_channel_full_pnl/get_all_channels_full_pnl return both *_msat and *_sats keys with the exact key names listed in the code — RPC response shape. Queries UNION forwards + daily_forwarding_stats(_inbound) with non-overlapping time predicates.

components.name: costs-closures-and-lifetime-accounting
components.responsibility: Capital accounting, all SAT-denominated: channel_costs (open cost, PK channel_id), rebalance_costs (cost_sats canonical + nullable cost_msat added later; append-only, never pruned), channel_closure_costs (close_type 'mutual'/'local_unilateral'/'remote_unilateral'/'unknown', closure/htlc_sweep/penalty fees, resolution_complete flag, bkpr_account for bookkeeper re-query), closed_channels (frozen full P&L snapshot at close: revenue, rebalance cost, net_pnl_sats, days_open, closer 'local'/'remote'/'mutual'/'unknown'), financial_snapshots (timestamp PK, node-level balances), lifetime_aggregates (singleton id=1: pruned_revenue_msat + pruned_forward_count preserved across pruning). get_confirmed_onchain_sats calls CLN listfunds and floors amount_msat of confirmed outputs.
components.rust_considerations: rebalance_costs is the budget-enforcement source of truth in cost_sats (budget math never reads cost_msat), while P&L reads COALESCE(cost_msat, cost_sats*1000) for precision — the dual-column msat migration split these consumers and both must survive. record_channel_closure/record_closed_channel_history freeze P&L at close time; remove_closed_channel_data deletes across many tables and returns per-table counts. Closure fields pass through _sanitize_fee (50k sat clamp).

components.name: peer-reputation-and-connection-history
components.responsibility: peer_reputation (PK peer_id, success/failure counts, multiplicative decay via decay_reputation), peer_connection_history (connect/disconnect event log pruned at the forwards cutoff, used for get_peer_uptime_percent), and hot_channel_protection_overrides (operator-managed peer list with optional min_depletion_trigger_pct REAL).
components.rust_considerations: Uptime percentage is derived by replaying the event log over a duration window — floating-point but low-stakes. Decay multiplies counts by decay_factor and stores integers (floor).

components.name: config-planner-lnplus-policies
components.responsibility: config_overrides (key/value TEXT with a monotonically bumped version counter used for cross-thread config invalidation), peer_policies CRUD (strategy default 'dynamic', rebalance_mode default 'enabled', fee_ppm_target, tags JSON, fee_multiplier_min/max, expires_at with delete_expired_policies), capacity planner tables (planner_candidates score REAL, planner_actions status lifecycle 'planned'→..., planner_recycle_ops close→open recycle state machine with funding_source/status/cycles_waited), LN+ swap ledger (lnplus_swaps status lifecycle with tag_added/incoming_tag_added contract-protection flags, lnplus_peers counters, lnplus_reserved_sats sum for budget interplay, prune terminal swaps >180d), dead_capital_stage, mempool_fee_history (sat_per_vbyte REAL for Vegas Reflex moving average).
components.rust_considerations: get_config_version/set_config_override version bump is a cross-thread cache-invalidation protocol — keep it atomic. tags and metadata_json columns are JSON TEXT blobs shared with other modules; preserve serialization. delete_expired_policies returns the list of expired peer rows (callers act on it). lnplus_reserved_sats feeds the same budget envelope as the reservation ledger.

external_dependencies: sqlite3 (stdlib; WAL mode, BEGIN IMMEDIATE, partial indexes, ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE, incremental_vacuum, busy_timeout)
threading (threading.local per-thread connections, Lock for connection registry)
pyln plugin object: plugin.log(level=debug/info/warn/error) and plugin.rpc.listfunds (only direct CLN RPC in this file)
CLN bookkeeper accounts (bkpr_account column stores hex channel_id for post-close sweep re-query; the RPC calls live elsewhere)
modules/utils.py: normalize_scid, base_to_sats_floor/ceil, base_delta_to_sats_toward_zero, sats_to_base
json, math, time, os (stdlib)
lens_maps.port_notes: Table inventory (name — purpose — key columns — unit): schema_version (singleton id=1, WRITE-ONLY, no version gating by design); channel_states (flow classification: channel_id PK, state source/sink/balanced, flow_ratio REAL, sats_in/sats_out SATS, + migrated confidence/velocity/flow_multiplier/ema_decay/kalman_* REALs, temporal_profile_json); fee_strategy_state (P&O fee controller: last_revenue_rate REAL, step_ppm, hysteresis fields, v2_state_json blob); fee_changes (audit, ppm, reason_code, 90d retention); rebalance_history (amount_sats/max_fee_sats/actual_fee_sats + actual_fee_msat, status incl. pending_settlement + payment_hash, 90d); forwards (MSAT-native hot table: in/out/fee_msat, resolution_time REAL, ~8d retention); channel_costs (open_cost_sats); rebalance_costs (cost_sats canonical + nullable cost_msat, never pruned — budget source of truth); channel_failures + pair_rebalance_failures (backoff/cooldowns, persisted); peer_reputation; peer_connection_history; lifetime_aggregates (singleton, pruned_revenue_MSAT); channel_probes; ignored_peers (legacy → migrated to peer_policies, renamed _backup_ignored_peers as marker); peer_policies; hot_channel_protection_overrides; config_overrides (versioned); mempool_fee_history; daily_forwarding_stats + daily_forwarding_stats_inbound (MSAT rollups, PK channel_id+UTC-midnight date); budget_reservations / spend_reservations / spend_events (SATS reservation ledger, statuses active/spent/released); financial_snapshots (SATS, timestamp PK); channel_closure_costs; closed_channels (frozen P&L, SATS); kalman_state (REAL filter state); plugin_flags; planner_candidates/planner_actions/planner_recycle_ops; lnplus_swaps/lnplus_peers; dead_capital_stage. UNIT CONVENTION (the sats→msat migration): forwards, daily_forwarding_stats(_inbound), lifetime_aggregates are msat-native; rebalance_costs and rebalance_history got ADDED msat columns (cost_msat, actual_fee_msat) alongside legacy sat columns — every reader COALESCEs msat first, sat*1000 fallback, and budget enforcement still reads cost_sats only; everything else (reservations, spend_events, snapshots, costs/closures, planner, LN+) is sats. Rounding at the reporting boundary is contractual: ceil for revenue/cost msat→sat, floor for volume, toward-zero for net deltas; ppm = fee_msat*1_000_000 // out_msat (integer). INVARIANTS a port must preserve exactly: (1) both reserve paths (_reserve_budget_atomic and reserve_spend) do the entire read-check-insert under BEGIN IMMEDIATE — the WAL single-writer lock IS the concurrency-correctness mechanism; committed total = active spend_reservations + active budget_reservations (both with NO time filter, P4-017) + windowed rebalance_costs + windowed spend_events; reject → ROLLBACK + (False, remaining); success remaining = min(daily, weekly) − amount. (2) Terminal reservation ids ('spent'/'released') are never resurrected; re-reserving an active id counts only the delta. (3) cleanup_old_data chunk atomicity: aggregate-into-daily-stats and delete-from-forwards happen in the same 2000-row transaction with additive ON CONFLICT upserts; day bucket = ts//86400*86400. (4) All migrations additive + idempotent, failures logged as 'DB migration warning: ...' and never fatal; schema_version never gates opening. (5) SCID alias matching (AxBxC, raw, A:B:C) on all read paths. (6) RPC-visible dict shapes: get_channel_full_pnl (*_msat and *_sats key pairs), get_budget_status, get_daily_rebalance_spend, get_spend_ledger_summary, get_rebalance_history_by_peer (fee_paid_msat, amount_msat), clear_all_reservations ({released_count/released_sats-style keys}), get_channel_revenue_totals (fees_earned_msat, volume_routed_msat, sourced_*). (7) Sanitizers clamp rather than error (fee ≤50k sats, |amount| ≤1e10 sats, NaN/inf→0). (8) spend_journal hooks fire only after successful commits and are exception-swallowed — never able to change an authorization outcome. (9) Shutdown must wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) before closing.

components.name: Config dataclass + validation tables
components.responsibility: Single mutable Config dataclass holding ~150 tunables with defaults, plus module-level validation tables: CONFIG_FIELD_TYPES (type map), CONFIG_FIELD_RANGES (inclusive numeric ranges), STRING_ENUM_VALID_VALUES (case-insensitive enums, normalized to lowercase), IMMUTABLE_CONFIG_KEYS ({db_path, dry_run}), PUBLIC_RUNTIME_KEYS (the ~70-key operator surface for revenue-config), DEPRECATED_RUNTIME_KEYS ({rebalance_min_profit} -> rebalance_hold_margin), and SHADOWED_SETTING_GATES (gate-flag -> gated keys for 4 feature families). __post_init__ enforces construction-time ranges on the drain fields and normalizes fee_profile/rebalance_router enums.
components.rust_considerations: Tables must stay in lockstep with struct fields (Python uses hasattr/getattr reflection; Rust needs a macro or codegen to map string keys to typed fields). Ranges are inclusive on both ends. Float fields must reject NaN/Infinity (math.isfinite). Enum matching is case-insensitive with lowercase normalization on store. classify_runtime_key returns exactly 'public'|'deprecated'|'internal'.

components.name: Startup override loading, risk-profile application, repair & detection
components.responsibility: Config.load_overrides(db): reads all persisted overrides, applies each via _apply_override (type-coerce, range-check, enum-check; failures become warnings, never errors), then applies risk-profile derived values ONLY to keys without explicit overrides (precedence: explicit DB override > profile bundle > dataclass default; resolution failures fail open to custom), then runs shadowed/deprecated detection against the explicitly-overridden key set, then cross-field invariant repairs each emitting a warning: min_fee_ppm clamped to max_fee_ppm; low_liquidity repaired to max(0, high-0.05); utilization floor repaired to max(0, ceiling-0.05); receivable floor repaired to target; lnplus_max_participants raised to min; daily>weekly budget is warn-only (no repair, 'the weekly cap binds first'). Returns the warning list for startup logging.
components.rust_considerations: Ordering is load-bearing: overrides -> profile derivation -> detection (against explicit keys only, pre-repair) -> repairs. Bool parsing at load: value.lower() in ('true','1','yes','on'). Out-of-range/invalid overrides are SKIPPED with a warning (field keeps default), not errors. Warning message strings are operator-facing and tested; preserve wording like 'Shadowed setting: {key} is set but {gate} is false — it has no effect until the gate is enabled' and 'Deprecated option {key} is set — it is a no-op; use {replacement}'.

components.name: Runtime update path (update_runtime + revenue-config RPC)
components.responsibility: Transactional runtime mutation: Validate (immutable-key check, unknown-key check, type parse with strict bool accepting true/1/yes/on and false/0/no/off else error, NaN/Inf reject, range check, enum check) -> under threading.Lock: cross-field checks (min/max fee, low/high liquidity, lnplus min/max participants, utilization floor/ceiling, sink/source threshold, receivable floor/target) -> DB write -> read-back verification (mismatch rolls back the DB row and returns 'Database write verification failed (Ghost Config prevention)') -> in-memory setattr + version bump. The revenue-config RPC (get/set/reset/list-mutable) gates set/reset to PUBLIC_RUNTIME_KEYS only; get on any attr works but attaches a warning for non-public keys and a classification field; reset deletes the override and tells the operator to restart.
components.rust_considerations: RPC response shapes are contracts: success = {status:'success', key, old_value, new_value, version}; every failure = {error: '<exact string>'} (not a JSON-RPC error). Error strings are checked by tests/operators, e.g. "Value {v} out of range [{min}, {max}] for {key}", "Key '{key}' cannot be changed at runtime", "Invalid boolean for {key}: {value!r} (use true/false)". Values are always passed to the DB as strings; typed value is what lands in memory. The lock scope (cross-field check + write + read-back + setattr) prevents TOCTOU and must be a single critical section in Rust.

components.name: ConfigSnapshot (immutable cycle view)
components.responsibility: Frozen dataclass mirroring Config; every worker cycle captures snapshot() at cycle start and reads only the snapshot, preventing torn reads mid-cycle. from_config copies fields under Config._lock, maps _version -> version, and falls back to the snapshot field's declared default when Config lacks the field (partial-deployment tolerance). History shows fields missing from the snapshot silently killed features in production (lnplus_*, min_fee_ppm_saturated were dead until mirrored).
components.rust_considerations: In Rust this collapses naturally to Arc<ConfigSnapshot> swapped atomically (arc-swap), which eliminates the whole missing-field bug class — but the port must guarantee snapshot completeness (derive/codegen from one struct, not two hand-maintained lists). Snapshot carries the version integer for audit trails.

components.name: Risk profiles
components.responsibility: risk_profile in {preserve, conservative, balanced, growth, custom}. FIELD_CLASSIFICATION assigns every Config field to exactly one of 7 categories (safety_invariant, authority_control, economic_risk, operational_timing, external_integration, advanced_expert, deprecated_transition); a test pin forces new fields to be classified. PROFILE_BUNDLES touch ONLY economic_risk keys (11 keys: budgets, hold margin, growth-budget knobs, planner ROI/open/close caps, lnplus preference margin). resolve_profile returns bundle minus explicit keys; unknown profiles resolve to {} (fail-conservative). preview_profile/preview_all give read-only restart-diffs with a daily>weekly contradiction pre-check.
components.rust_considerations: Pure data + set arithmetic; trivial to port. Invariants to preserve exactly: 'custom' and unknown names derive nothing; 'conservative' restates dataclass defaults; growth_budget_hard_ceiling_sats (safety) never appears in any bundle; profiles apply at STARTUP only (a runtime risk_profile set takes effect at restart); preview never mutates and returns the documented shape including the 'activation' hint string.

components.name: Plugin option registration + dynamic setconfig handlers
components.responsibility: ~120 unique 'revenue-ops-*' plugin options registered via plugin.add_option (240 grep hits incl. descriptions), kebab-case names mapping to snake_case Config fields, all defaults as strings. A subset is dynamic=True with on_change callbacks that validate and raise ValueError to reject a bad lightning-cli setconfig: _on_rebalance_tuning_change (rebalance_hold_margin 0.0-1000.0, pair_fee_cap_ppm >=0) and a rebalance-router flip validator. Separately, _refresh_dynamic_config polls listconfigs at the top of each boltz/planner/lnplus cycle to bridge setconfig persistence into in-memory objects for options without on_change (boltz enforce-budget/daily-budget, planner_execute_closes, all 12 lnplus keys, min_fee_ppm_saturated).
components.rust_considerations: CRITICAL precedence rule the port must keep: an active revenue-config DB override on a field WINS over the listconfigs/setconfig view — the refresh loop skips any field with a live DB override (observed production bugs when this was missing: planner closes re-enabled, str option stomped 1 minute after set). Refresh bool parse is ('true','1','yes') — note it lacks 'on', unlike load_overrides; preserve or consciously unify. Empty value_str skips numeric/bool casts but IS applied for str casts (clearability fix C-1(b)). on_change rejection is via raised error so lightningd refuses the setconfig; in Rust (cln-plugin) this maps to returning Err from the option callback.

components.name: Chain-cost floor + liquidity buckets helpers
components.responsibility: ChainCostDefaults: constants (open 5000 sats, close 3000, lifetime 365d, 1M sats/day volume) and calculate_floor_ppm(capacity, opener) — remote-opened channels exclude the open cost; result max(1, int(floor)). LiquidityBuckets: 7-bucket classification of outbound_ratio with fee multipliers 3.0/2.0/1.25/1.0/0.85/0.7/0.5 (unknown bucket -> 1.0).
components.rust_considerations: float division then int() truncation (toward zero) then max(1,...) — replicate truncation, not rounding. Bucket boundaries are strict '<' comparisons in ascending order.

components.name: Config file templates + validation harness config
components.responsibility: Documentation-grade sample lightningd config files (full: 482 lines with every option commented; minimal: 51 lines) and revenue_validation.yaml, which configures the daily production-validation harness (node transport 'ssh lnnode', lightning-cli prefix, log-extract grep, rollback thresholds: 3 plugin restarts/24h, 25% revenue drop, 50% rebalance success floor). Not code, but the option names must match the Rust plugin's registered options exactly.
depends_on: Plugin option registration + dynamic setconfig handlers
components.rust_considerations: Templates must be regenerated for the Rust binary but keep identical option names so existing operator config files load unchanged. revenue_validation.yaml is consumed by scripts/, not the plugin — out of plugin-port scope but its log_extract_command greps for specific log prefixes (FEE:, REBALANCE_FLOOR, etc.) the Rust port should keep emitting.

components.name: Econ artifact JSON schemas
components.responsibility: Versioned JSON Schemas for the economic-governance refactor artifacts: intent envelopes, economic snapshots, ledger events/projections, and conformance test cases. These define the serialization contract the econ_* gated subsystems write to econ_ledger.db and that conformance/golden tests validate against.
components.rust_considerations: Rust structs (serde) must serialize to shapes that validate against these exact schemas — they are the cross-implementation conformance boundary for the port (golden/expected-projections tests exist under tests/). Version fields (v0/v1) must be preserved; do not collapse versions.

external_dependencies: CLN RPC: listconfigs (dynamic config refresh bridge)
CLN RPC: setconfig via pyln dynamic=True options + on_change callbacks
pyln-client Plugin (add_option, @plugin.method, plugin.log)
SQLite via modules/database.py: config_overrides table (get_all_config_overrides, get/set/delete_config_override, get_config_version)
Python stdlib: threading.Lock, dataclasses, math.isfinite
Boltz client CLI (boltz-* options: cli-path, datadir, wallets, sudo)
lightningnetwork.plus API (lnplus_* knobs)
askrene/xpay CLN plugins (askrene_layer, askrene_layers, rebalance_router='v3')
lens_maps.lens: Configuration system: options, runtime mutation, snapshots, validation, risk profiles
lens_maps.port_notes: Behaviors a Rust port must preserve exactly. (1) RPC shapes: revenue-config failures return {\"error\": \"<msg>\"} dicts, never RPC errors; success set returns {status:'success', key, old_value, new_value, version}; error strings are exact contracts (\"Value {v} out of range [{min}, {max}] for {key}\", \"Key '{key}' is not a public runtime control\", \"Unknown config key: {key}\", \"Database write verification failed (Ghost Config prevention)\", \"Invalid boolean for {key}: {value!r} (use true/false)\"). (2) Three-layer precedence, highest first: explicit DB override (revenue-config set) > risk-profile bundle (startup-only, economic_risk keys only) > dataclass default; and at runtime a live DB override beats the listconfigs/setconfig view in _refresh_dynamic_config — dropping this guard caused real incidents. (3) Budget semantics: daily_budget_sats is a fixed floor, weekly_budget_sats a hard ceiling that binds first; daily>weekly is warn-only, never repaired. diagnostic_rebalance_max_fee_sats is clamped at use to [1, min(daily_budget_sats, 10000)] and derives the shock ppm ceiling as ceil(cap/amount*1e6). growth_budget_hard_ceiling_sats is a safety key excluded from all profile bundles. min_fee_ppm_saturated only ever LOWERS the floor for saturated/source channels (values >= min_fee_ppm ignored at use; 0 = allow cheap egress); other floors still compose via max(). (4) Validation asymmetry: startup _apply_override SKIPS bad values with warnings; runtime update_runtime REJECTS with errors; bool token sets differ (load: true/1/yes/on; refresh: true/1/yes; update_runtime: strict two-sided). Ranges inclusive; floats reject NaN/Inf; string enums case-insensitive, stored lowercase. (5) Cross-field checks (min/max fee, low/high liquidity, sink/source, utilization floor/ceiling, lnplus min/max participants, receivable floor/target) run inside the write lock at runtime and as repair-with-warning at startup; the write path is validate -> DB write -> read-back verify (rollback on mismatch) -> setattr -> version bump, all in one critical section. (6) ConfigSnapshot: immutable per-cycle view captured under lock; every field must be mirrored or the feature silently dies (historical C1/M-27/E-2 bugs) — generate snapshot from the Config struct, never hand-mirror. (7) Immutable keys {db_path, dry_run} can never change at runtime (dry_run immutability is a safety property: enabling it live could hide actions). (8) Deprecated shim rebalance_min_profit must still parse/echo (classification 'deprecated') but is enforced nowhere; replacement rebalance_hold_margin is denominated in SATS of expected net value (range 0-1000). (9) Option names are 'revenue-ops-<kebab>' mapping to snake_case fields; all option defaults are strings; existing operator config files must load unchanged. Operator-facing knob groups: core/safety (paused, dry_run, db_path, authority_level, risk_profile, min_wallet_reserve, rpc_timeout_seconds, reservation_timeout_hours, max_concurrent_jobs, flow/fee/rebalance intervals); fees (min/max_fee_ppm, min_fee_ppm_saturated, base_fee_msat, base_fee_policy, fee_profile, market_fee_mode, neighbor_median_min_competitors, fee_market_boundary_* x6, inbound_fee_estimate_ppm, thompson_prior_std_fee, enable_vegas_reflex, vegas_decay_rate, enable_dynamic_htlcmax + htlcmax_{source,sink,balanced}_pct); flow classification (flow_window_days, source/sink_threshold, low/high_liquidity_threshold, htlc_congestion_threshold, enable_reputation, reputation_decay, enable_kelly); budgets (daily/weekly_budget_sats, growth_budget_* x5, diagnostic_rebalance_max_fee_sats, allow_zero_cost_auto_rebalance_when_budget_zero); rebalance (rebalance_max_amount, cooldown_hours, emergency_local_ratio, drift_override_ratio, hold_margin, pair_fee_cap_ppm, activity_* x3, utilization_* x4, size_* x3, rebalance_router, askrene_layer/layers, hot_channel_protection_* x7, deprecated rebalance_min_profit); planner/capex (planner_* x13, capex_* x8, estimated_open_cost_sats); Boltz/drain (boltz_auto_cycle_* x4, boltz_structural_budget_sats_per_day, receivable_ratio_target/floor, drain_fee_discount_max, node_drain_bias_enabled/max, plus main-file-only boltz-* options: enabled, cli-path, datadir, btc/lbtc wallets, daily-budget, enforce-budget, max-withdraw, routing-fee-limit-ppm, sudo-user/use-sudo, timeout); expansion treasury (x7); LN+ (x13 incl. swaps_enabled gate); econ governance flags (econ_shadow, econ_governor_{rebalance,planner,lnplus,boltz,fees}, econ_arbiter, econ_cycle_{rebalance,planner,boltz}, econ_ev_populated, econ_conflict_rules_extended — all default off; flipping econ_ev_populated changes J3 batch-arbitration ORDER). Shadowed-setting gates to warn on: growth_budget_enabled, fee_market_boundary_enabled, lnplus_swaps_enabled, expansion_treasury_enabled.

components.name: plugin_entrypoint_orchestrator
components.responsibility: CLN plugin entrypoint: registers all revenue-* RPCs, options, hooks, and background loops (fee cycle, rebalance cycle, planner, Boltz, LN+ watcher, telemetry publishers). Wires every subsystem together and owns the operator surface (revenue-status, revenue-fee-debug, revenue-rebalance-debug, revenue-config get/set/reset/list-mutable).
components.rust_considerations: Port target is a CLN plugin over stdin/stdout JSON-RPC (cln-plugin crate). Python version uses pyln-client with async background loops; Rust needs tokio task equivalents plus revenue-wake-all semantics. RPC response shapes of the three read-only surfaces are contract: they must return JSON with no external plugins present and must never call mutation RPCs. revenue-config set writes DB overrides that win over setconfig/config-file on every dynamic refresh; unknown keys must return the exact error 'not a public runtime control'.

components.name: economic_governance_core
components.responsibility: The governed core every economic mutation flows through: canonical immutable EconomicSnapshot -> typed intents (idempotency key, EV, cost cap, snapshot ref) -> arbiter (duplicate/stale/conflicting-intent rejection with stable reason codes) -> governor (fail-closed: pause gate, authority_level gate, conflict registry, staleness, atomic budget reservation) -> execution -> append-only econ_ledger.db with hourly self-reconciliation. Also the deterministic shadow cycle (revenue-econ-cycle) used for Rust parity testing.
components.rust_considerations: This is the layer explicitly designed for the Rust port: wire contracts are versioned language-neutral JSON schemas in schemas/ (economic_snapshot and intent v1 FROZEN closed objects; ledger_event.v0, ledger_projection.v0, conformance_case.v0) with a 40-scenario conformance corpus in tests/conformance/scenarios/. Refactor invariants 14-16 apply: no dict/set iteration-order dependence, no binary float in monetary authorization (checked integer/fixed-point only), no wall-clock reads or unseeded randomness inside decisions; same snapshot+config+clock must yield identical structured decisions in Python and Rust. Ledger is append-only SQLite; replay reconstructs state.

components.name: fee_controller
components.responsibility: Fee decisioning per channel. DTS+PID controller is authoritative per ADR-001 with fixed post-processing order: rails -> rate-limit -> deadband -> cooldown. Includes auto fee bands (default on, manual policy bands as fallback), congestion handling, zero-fee probe with 24h TTL, drain-fee-discount bias, and dynamic htlc_max scaled by flow class and live-depletion-capped to a fraction of spendable balance (10k-sat clamp floor) with a gossip-churn deadband before setchannel broadcast.
components.rust_considerations: PID/DTS math involves floats — acceptable for the decision value but the pipeline ordering (rails, rate-limit, deadband, cooldown) is the frozen ADR-001 contract and revenue-fee-debug must distinguish policy vs arbitration vs governor rejection with stable reason codes. Historical audit found decision-priority ordering bugs (Congestion > Zero-Fee Probe > Thompson/HC); a port must reproduce actual code order, not documented order. htlc_max broadcasts are economic mutations that flow through the governor. Deprecated no-op keys (fee_market_boundary_*, rebalance_min_profit) must remain accepted no-ops until the 2026-08-12 compatibility window closes.

components.name: rebalance_stack
components.responsibility: Circular rebalance decisioning and execution: pair selection, sats-EV gating with rebalance_hold_margin, route discovery pinned to the v3/askrene router path, and live execution of the explicit askrene-priced route via native sendpay/waitsendpay (no Sling). Failed route segments recorded as local observations and published to datastore key [revenue, segment-observations]. Hot-channel protection widens budget/shortens cooldown for fast-draining high-profit channels.
components.rust_considerations: Requires the full askrene layer lifecycle (askrene-create-layer/update-channel/bias-channel/remove-layer) — this is what pins the CLN v24.11.1 floor. Zero-fee rule: zero pair budget must block even zero-fee routes unless allow_zero_cost_auto_rebalance_when_budget_zero is set (a known historical safety gap, now a contract). Suppression honesty (RB-H3): capital-control-suppressed periods must show no fabricated activity. Fee persistence is msat-native (actual_fee_msat in rebalance_history, cost_msat in rebalance_costs, sat fields derived once at write). Async sendpay/waitsendpay orchestration with recovery after restart must be idempotent.

components.name: profitability_flow_analysis
components.responsibility: Msat-native per-channel economics: channel valuation = max(exit_fees, sourced_fees), fleet revenue = exit fees only (no double count), inbound-gateway protection, ZOMBIE/STAGNANT classification on total forward count (exit + sourced), source/sink/router demand-flow classification from FlowMetrics and gossip heuristics. Publishes the canonical profitability snapshot to datastore key [revenue, profitability-summary].
components.rust_considerations: Rounding rules are contract and must be bit-exact: balances/capacity floor to sats, revenue ceils to sats (sub-satoshi earnings stay visible), costs/budgets ceil, signed net deltas round toward zero (-1 msat must not become -1 sat). The datastore payload shape (timestamp, channels[]; per-channel msat fields, counters, class, roi_pct, role, fee_multiplier) is the frozen cross-plugin contract in REVENUE_PROFITABILITY_SUMMARY_CONTRACT.md. One classification authority per snapshot — no channel may be classified differently by two subsystems in a cycle.

components.name: capacity_planner_capex
components.responsibility: Channel-open intelligence and capital controls: portfolio balance governor (hard gate blocking outbound opens at >95% local, constrained 85-95%), multi-strategy candidate discovery (winner/demand-flow/route-pair/graph/neighbor) with 0-1 score normalization and pool slot quotas, capital hurdle (planner_min_annual_roi_pct, default 1%), capital recycling (close-and-reopen), dual-fund via fundchannel request_amt when peers advertise option_will_fund. Publishes [revenue, capex-summary] telemetry.
components.rust_considerations: Planner closes are recommendation-only by default; live closes require revenue-ops-planner-execute-closes=true AND a positive max-closes-per-cycle — a port must preserve fail-closed defaults. _calculate_open_ev is shared with LN+ swap scoring, so its numeric behavior must match across both consumers. capex-summary telemetry explicitly cannot authorize spend. Score normalization must be deterministic (no dict-order dependence in candidate ranking).

components.name: boltz_manager
components.responsibility: Lightning<->on-chain swap integration (17 options, 22 RPCs): treasury-mode-first auto-cycle maintaining a confirmed on-chain reserve, balance cycle fallback, expansion treasury (reverse-swaps excess local liquidity to build the on-chain reserve the planner needs), and the drain-demand structural loop-out credit capped by revenue-ops-boltz-structural-budget-sats per day (default 0 = off).
components.rust_considerations: External Boltz HTTP API with swap-protocol crypto (preimages, refund/claim scripts) — needs careful async HTTP + on-chain script handling in Rust. Budget honesty caveat is documented: the structural envelope is enforced from RECORDED spend, so a swap whose fee fails to record does not deplete it — a port should preserve (or explicitly improve via approved migration) this semantic. lnplus_reserved_sats is subtracted before the confirmed-on-chain calculation (LN+ is a third consumer of the same reserve pool). Rebalancing and Boltz have separate budgets by design; the circular rebalancer always gets first claim before structural loop-out.

components.name: lnplus_swaps
components.responsibility: Autonomous join-only participation in lightningnetwork.plus liquidity-swap rings: EV scoring against best regular open plus preference margin, hard eligibility floors (min rank 8/gold fail-closed on missing rank, positive-ratings floor, 3-4 participants, max 3-month duration), one swap in flight per node, and an obligations watcher that drives connect -> fundchannel (deadline-escalating feerate) -> complete_application -> no_close activation -> rating and release at contract end.
components.rust_considerations: Obligation semantics are hard invariants: an accepted swap is a debt — fulfillment is exempt from pause/authority_level gates but never from authorization or the ledger; disabling stops new applications but in-flight swaps are honored to completion; the one-strike circuit breaker (missed 48h deadline or ledger/LN+ divergence) blocks only NEW commitments and never self-clears (operator-only revenue-lnplus-breaker-clear). revenue-lnplus-backfill must stay idempotent (existing rows never touched). Missing/zero peer rank must fail closed, not pass. State machine must survive plugin restarts mid-obligation.

components.name: database_persistence
components.responsibility: SQLite persistence layer for everything: forwards/flow history, rebalance_history and rebalance_costs (msat-native), budget reservations, policy/config DB overrides, LN+ swap ledger, Boltz records, hive-era grace tables, plus the separate append-only econ_ledger.db. Owns schema migrations and the reservation store the governor reconciles against hourly.
components.rust_considerations: Largest single module. Rust port must read the EXISTING SQLite schemas in place (rusqlite) — migration compatibility, not greenfield. Reservation state machine (available -> reserved -> spent | released, ambiguous outcomes quarantined until reconciled) must restore across restarts without double-spend; missing cost records must conservatively consume the reservation or block spending — never produce free budget. Python sqlite3 implicit transaction behavior differs from rusqlite defaults; audit each write path's transactional boundary. sat fields are derived from msat once at write time, not recomputed.

components.name: config_policy_layer
components.responsibility: Runtime configuration and operator controls: paused, daily_budget_sats, fee rails, authority_level (observe < fees < liquidity < capital), risk_profile bundles (preserve/conservative/balanced/growth/custom with revenue-profile-preview diff), econ_* flag gates with per-capability rollback, DB-override precedence over setconfig/config-file, per-channel policy bands (revenue-policy), and the optional growth-budget uplift (profit-fraction + experiment-fraction, bounded by max-extra and hard ceiling).
components.rust_considerations: Override precedence is contract: a DB override set via revenue-config set wins over setconfig/file on every dynamic refresh until revenue-config reset; the reset response must say whether the field applies immediately or needs restart. list-mutable defines the exact public-key set; non-listed keys return 'not a public runtime control'. Governance keys (econ_*, authority_level, risk_profile) are runtime-only, never config-file options. Deprecated keys must remain accepted no-ops through 2026-08-12. authority_level default is capital (permissive); operators dial DOWN.

components.name: data_and_protection_services
