components.responsibility: Shared read-side services: CLN state collection (listpeerchannels, forwards, gossip) feeding snapshot construction, and the protection service that centralizes channel protections (no_close contract tags, inbound-gateway and hot-channel protection) consulted by planner, rebalancer, and fee paths.
components.rust_considerations: Must be strictly read-only (AGENTS.md: data collection is read-only; read-only surfaces never call mutation RPCs). Missing/stale/malformed evidence must lower confidence or block action — never be treated as zero cost/zero risk/authorization. Handle absent RPC fields, private/unannounced channels, opening/closing states, and inactive peers without crashing (required-test categories).

external_dependencies: Core Lightning JSON-RPC v24.11.1+ (hard floor: full askrene layer lifecycle — askrene-create-layer/update-channel/bias-channel/remove-layer; v24.08.1 explicitly unsupported despite having getroutes); tested pass on v24.11.1, v25.02.2, v25.05, v25.09.3, v25.12

CLN RPCs used: getinfo, listpeerchannels, listforwards, gossip queries, setchannel (fee + htlc_max), getroutes, sendpay/waitsendpay, fundchannel (incl. request_amt dual-fund), close, connect, datastore/listdatastore, setconfig
CLN datastore telemetry keys produced: [revenue, profitability-summary], [revenue, capex-summary], [revenue, segment-observations]
bookkeeper plugin (recommended, optional — P&L/cost accounting)
Boltz HTTP API (submarine + reverse swaps)
lightningnetwork.plus (LN+) HTTP API (create_application, complete_application, ratings, swap listings)
Python libs: pyln-client (==24.11.1 in smoke tests), sqlite3, requests/HTTP client per requirements.txt
SQLite databases: main plugin DB + append-only econ_ledger.db
No Sling plugin (explicit non-dependency); no hive/mycelium coordinator (removed 2026-07, pinned by tests/test_architecture_guard.py)
lens_maps.lens: architecture-and-invariants map of cl_revenue_ops (Python CLN plugin, ~58k LOC) for a Rust port: governed economic core, module boundaries per docs/planning/refactor.md, hard budget/zero-fee/capital invariants, CLN compatibility floor
lens_maps.port_notes: The repo was deliberately refactored to be language-portable: port against the frozen wire contracts, not the Python code. (1) Contracts: schemas/ holds economic_snapshot and intent v1 (FROZEN, closed objects), ledger_event.v0, ledger_projection.v0, conformance_case.v0; tests/conformance/scenarios/ has 40 scenario classes validated by tools/conformance/validate_fixtures.py with no plugin imports — a Rust build should pass this corpus byte-for-byte, and refactor.md prescribes Rust-in-shadow-mode with parity tests before cutover. (2) Determinism invariants 14-16: decisions must not depend on dict/set iteration order, float behavior in monetary authorization (checked integer/fixed-point only), wall-clock reads, or unseeded randomness; same snapshot+config+clock => identical structured decisions. (3) Budget honesty: reservation state machine available->reserved->spent|released; reserve worst-case cost before execution; ambiguous outcomes quarantine until reconciled; a missing cost record must conservatively consume the reservation or block spending — never free budget; restarts restore reservations without double-spend; hourly ledger-vs-reservation reconciliation (revenue-econ-reconcile). Known documented soft spot: the Boltz structural envelope depletes from RECORDED spend only. (4) Zero-fee/zero-budget rule: zero rebalance budget blocks even zero-fee routes unless allow_zero_cost_auto_rebalance_when_budget_zero; zero-fee probes carry a 24h TTL. (5) Capital controls: authority_level ladder observe<fees<liquidity<capital (default capital); governor is fail-closed in fixed order pause -> authority -> conflict registry -> staleness -> atomic reservation; planner closes recommendation-only by default; LN+ obligations exempt from pause/authority but never authorization/ledger; LN+ breaker never self-clears; one LN+ swap in flight; missing peer rank fails closed; max 3-month capital lockup. (6) Money: msat-native internally, convert only at boundaries with exact rounding (balances/capacity floor, revenue ceil, costs/budgets ceil, signed net toward zero). (7) Fee controller: ADR-001 DTS+PID authoritative, post-order rails->rate-limit->deadband->cooldown is contract; debug surfaces must distinguish policy vs arbitration vs governor rejection via stable reason codes. (8) Exact strings/shapes to preserve: 'not a public runtime control' error, the three datastore telemetry payload shapes (contracts in docs/contracts/), revenue-status/fee-debug/rebalance-debug JSON that must work with no external plugins and never trigger mutations, deprecated no-op keys (fee_market_boundary_*, rebalance_min_profit) accepted until 2026-08-12 per docs/refactor/phase0/contract-compatibility-policy.md. (9) Consumers must treat stale/missing/malformed telemetry as unknown confidence, never zero value or an action command; internally missing evidence lowers confidence or blocks — never authorizes. (10) Single-plugin process: do NOT split into services; SQLite schemas must be adopted in place (existing DBs, sat-derived-once-at-write columns).

components.name: ThreadSafeRpcProxy + socket-timeout guard
components.responsibility: Wraps pyln's rpc object so every RPC runs on a bounded 16-worker ThreadPoolExecutor with a caller-side future.result(proxy_timeout). A separate 4-worker pool serves fire_and_forget() pushes. A monkeypatch on pyln UnixSocket.connect applies a per-thread socket recv timeout (proxy_timeout + 5.0s buffer) as a backstop that frees wedged workers (P1-007).
components.rust_considerations: Rust port replaces the pyln monkeypatch with a native socket read timeout — but must preserve the two-tier semantics: caller-side timeout fires FIRST at proxy_timeout (default 30s, or config.rpc_timeout_seconds, default 15 in Config), socket backstop at proxy_timeout+5.0s. Long-poll methods (any name starting with 'wait': wait, waitanyinvoice, waitinvoice, waitsendpay, waitblockheight, waitrune) get NO socket timeout. Caller 'timeout' kwarg extends proxy ceiling to max(proxy_timeout, timeout+5); '_proxy_timeout' kwarg is stripped before reaching CLN; 'timeout' on call() is consumed, never sent to lightningd. Backpressure: main pool semaphore of 16+32 slots acquired with min(max(proxy_timeout,0.1),1.0)s timeout -> RPCOverloadedError; async pool 4+64 slots, non-blocking acquire, drops with warn log. NO retries anywhere and NO circuit breaker (deliberately removed). Error types: RPCTimeoutError extends RpcError with message 'RPC timeout for method: {method}'; RPCOverloadedError message 'RPC worker pool saturated for method: {method}'; RPCBreakerOpen kept as alias subclass. Shutdown: _bounded_executor_shutdown drains on a daemon thread joined with 10.0s timeout (P1-029). Executor internal param must not be named 'timeout' (waitsendpay kwarg collision — see test_rpc_proxy_timeout_collision.py).

components.name: DataService (tiered RPC cache)
components.responsibility: Unified data-access layer over the RPC proxy with tiered TTL caching. All modules call DataService instead of plugin.rpc directly. Also hosts the datastore_push envelope helper and askrene adapter methods.
components.rust_considerations: Exact TTL tiers to preserve: FOREVER (getinfo-derived node_id/network/alias, listconfigs; fetched once under lock, never evicted, separate store), TTL_LONG=300s (listnodes per node_id, feerates per style), TTL_MEDIUM=30s (listpeerchannels broadcast, listfunds, listpeers, listchannels keyed 'listchannels:{source}:{destination}:{scid}', listforwards keyed per status, listclosedchannels, getinfo:blockheight). NEVER cached: per-peer listpeerchannels(peer_id), askrene-listlayers (shared mutable cross-plugin state — caching it is explicitly a bug), getroute/getroutes, all payment lifecycle and mutation calls. Mutations invalidate specific keys: setchannel->listpeerchannels; fundchannel/close->listfunds+listpeerchannels; connect->listpeers; askrene-create/remove-layer->askrene-listlayers. Cache cap _CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES=256, evict oldest-by-timestamp when exceeded; stale entries deleted on read. Two locks: _lock for TTL cache, _forever_lock for forever tier (fetch happens INSIDE the forever lock — serializes first getinfo). get_routes strips 'timeout' kwarg and forwards it to proxy call() only when not None. datastore_push: rejects non-dict, rejects payloads containing 'error' key, injects int(time.time()) 'timestamp' if absent, rejects >60000 UTF-8 bytes (warn log 'Datastore payload too large for {key}: {n} bytes'), mode='create-or-replace', never raises, returns bool.

components.name: utils (msat/base-unit helpers)
components.responsibility: Dependency-light shared helpers: SCID normalization and msat parsing/conversion with directional rounding rules used for all money math across the plugin.
components.rust_considerations: parse_msat must accept: objects with .millisatoshis (pyln Millisatoshi — in Rust, a serde variant), int/float, strings with optional 'msat' suffix (stripped), and return 0 on None, bool (explicit U-1 fix: bool is never valid), or any parse failure — never error. normalize_scid replaces ':' with 'x' and maps None->''. Rounding contracts are load-bearing: base_to_sats_ceil (-(-x//1000)) for fees/budgets/costs AND revenue reporting (sub-sat earnings stay visible); base_to_sats_floor for capacity/balances (never overstate spendable); base_delta_to_sats_toward_zero for signed deltas. BASE_UNITS_PER_SAT=1000 is a named constant by design. Permanent aliases (parse_base_unit, msat_to_sats_ceil, MSAT_PER_SAT, etc.) exist for callers.

components.name: Daemon loop scheduler + heartbeat liveness
components.responsibility: Eight daemon threads started at init, each a while-loop gated on shutdown_event.wait(interval) with staggered startup delays and per-loop crash backoff. A heartbeat registry feeds revenue-health with per-loop stall detection.
components.rust_considerations: Threads (all daemon=True, named): flow-analysis (startup wait 30s), fee-adjustment (90s), rebalance-check (180s), startup-snapshot (one-shot delayed), financial-snapshot (300s), boltz-auto-cycle (computed startup delay), capacity-planner, lnplus-watcher (180s stagger, interval +/- random jitter). Every loop: on exception, log and shutdown_event.wait(_LOOP_BACKOFF_SECONDS); loops must exit promptly when shutdown_event is set (SIGTERM -> event.set()) — in Rust use a CancellationToken/condvar wait, not sleep. Heartbeats recorded per tick with time.monotonic(): stall threshold is 3x the loop's own interval, floor 900s, fallback _LOOP_STALL_SECONDS=3600 when no interval reported; one-shot loops report state 'complete' and are never stalled. This liveness snapshot is an operator-visible RPC surface (revenue-health) whose shape tests assert (test_loop_heartbeat_surface.py).

components.name: Database connection layer (threading-relevant slice)
components.responsibility: SQLite access with per-thread connections tracked under _thread_conn_lock, opened with isolation_level=None (autocommit; explicit BEGIN for transactions) and check_same_thread=False so close_all_connections() can close them from the shutdown path.
components.rust_considerations: In Rust, rusqlite with a per-thread connection pool or a single connection behind a mutex; preserve autocommit-by-default with explicit transaction scopes. The Python code relies on per-thread connections because 8+ loops plus 16 RPC-adjacent workers touch the DB; a Rust port should preserve the property that a wedged writer cannot deadlock shutdown (connections closable from another thread).

components.name: Test harness (mock pyln + tmp sqlite)
components.responsibility: Pytest fixtures: pyln.client is replaced in sys.modules with a MagicMock BEFORE module imports (mock_pyln.Plugin = MagicMock, RpcError = Exception); temp_db_path yields a tempfile-backed sqlite path deleted on teardown; mock_rpc pre-stubs getinfo/listchannels/listfunds/listpeers/plugin('list') with canonical empty shapes. An autouse fixture monkeypatches CapacityPlanner.__init__ and RebalanceEngine.__init__ to inject a PermissivePolicyManager when none is set (policy gates fail closed by design).
components.rust_considerations: Rust equivalent: a trait for the RPC surface (so tests inject a fake) plus tmpfile sqlite. The autouse permissive-policy-manager fixture encodes a production invariant to keep: policy gates FAIL CLOSED when policy_manager is None. Key behavior tests to port as the conformance suite: test_rpc_proxy_timeout_collision.py, test_p1_007_rpc_socket_timeout.py, test_p1_029_bounded_shutdown.py, test_data_service.py, test_utils.py, test_msat_accounting_regressions.py, test_loop_heartbeat_threshold.py, test_daemon_survival.py, test_gossip_cache.py.

external_dependencies: pyln-client (Plugin, LightningRpc, Millisatoshi, RpcError; UnixSocket.connect is monkeypatched)

CLN RPCs: getinfo, listconfigs, listpeerchannels, listfunds, listpeers, listchannels, listforwards, listclosedchannels, listnodes, feerates, setchannel, fundchannel, close, connect, signmessage, getroute, getroutes (askrene), invoice, sendpay, waitsendpay, delpay, delinvoice, pay, listpays, decode, bkpr-inspect, bkpr-listaccountevents, askrene-listlayers/create-layer/remove-layer/update-channel/bias-node/bias-channel/disable-node/age/inform-channel/reserve/unreserve, datastore, plugin list/listplugins
sqlite3 (stdlib; per-thread conns, isolation_level=None, check_same_thread=False)
Python stdlib: threading, concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor, time, json, random
pytest + unittest.mock (tests only)
lens_maps.lens: rpc-proxy, data-service caching, msat helpers, threading model, test harness
lens_maps.port_notes: Behaviors a Rust port must preserve exactly. (1) Timeout layering: default proxy timeout is config.rpc_timeout_seconds (Config default 15, valid range 1-300; hardcoded fallback 30 when config is None). Caller timeout kwarg extends the ceiling to max(proxy, int(timeout)+5); socket backstop = proxy_timeout + 5.0s; wait* methods never get a socket timeout; proxy_timeout<=0 disables the socket backstop. There are NO retries and NO circuit breaker — a timed-out call raises RPCTimeoutError immediately and the semantics are one-shot. (2) Exact error strings (tests and callers match on them): 'RPC timeout for method: {method}', 'RPC worker pool saturated for method: {method}', log lines 'RPC timeout after {N}s on {method}' (warn) and 'RPC pool saturated on {method} (submission queue full)' (warn), 'fire_and_forget {m} dropped (async queue full, streak={n})'. RPCTimeoutError must be an RpcError subclass so existing catch sites work; RPCBreakerOpen remains a catchable alias. (3) Backpressure budgets: main pool 16 workers + 32 queued (semaphore 48, acquire timeout clamp min(max(proxy,0.1),1.0)); async pool 4 workers + 64 queued, non-blocking acquire, failed-push streak counter reset to 0 on any success, guarded by its own lock. (4) Cache contract: TTLs FOREVER/-1, 300, 30; key formats include None as the literal string 'None' in f-string keys (e.g. 'listchannels:None:None:None') — cache keys must match or hit rates silently change; 256-entry cap with oldest-by-ts eviction; expired entries evicted on read; invalidate(None) clears only the TTL tier, never the forever tier; askrene-listlayers must never be cached (cross-plugin mutable state). (5) RPC response shapes are pyln dicts passed through untouched — DataService returns raw CLN JSON (e.g. {'channels': [...]}, {'peers': [...]}); msat fields may arrive as int, 'Nmsat' string, or Millisatoshi, and parse_msat's total function (0 on any failure, bool rejected) is depended on for accounting. (6) Money rounding: ceil for fees/budgets/costs and revenue reporting, floor for capacity/balances, toward-zero for signed deltas — regression-tested in test_msat_accounting_regressions.py. (7) Threading: 8 named daemon loops with staggered startups (30/90/180/300s...), all sleeps via shutdown_event.wait(interval) so SIGTERM exits immediately; per-loop exception backoff; heartbeat registry using monotonic time with stall = 3x own interval, floor 900s, fallback 3600s; bounded 10s executor drain at shutdown. (8) datastore_push envelope: adds timestamp if missing, refuses dicts containing 'error', refuses >60000 UTF-8 bytes, mode create-or-replace, never raises. (9) Logging convention: plugin.log(msg, level='info'|'warn'|'debug'), module prefixes like 'KALMAN:' and truncated ids '{id[:12]}...'; logging failures are swallowed (try/except around log calls in hot/shutdown paths). (10) SCIDs normalized to 'x' separator internally; CLN may return ':' forms.
